“Jobling, there are chords in the human mind.”
Charles Dickens, Bleak House
Meaning by the standard of repetitions and patterns is inherent in the universe. Just take life itself. It is at least purposeful, plants grow toward the sun, animals grow and evolve and grow up, and more have the need to reproduce and be productive than not. It is only with a farther perspective, access to knowledge, intuitively or thought or in epiphany, that, as Searle noted, the intentionality of thought can create meaning and limit its forms, which are possibly endless, and therefore we are the creators of the limits of our forms of thought, and can think accordingly. If the limits hold, evince, display and exhibit infinite possibilities then there are in fact no limits to the possibilities of the mind, accept insofar as the world and universe of which it is conscious confines the mind to its body and world and universe it was born out of and resides in. In either case, this subtle instinct or genetic coding or awareness in the undercurrent leads of course to less limits on the intellect. And the fewer the limits, and the more capacity of mind, the more individual consciousnesses are responsible for creating their own meaning.
There are many ways to create meaning in our lives. We can transcend ourselves by several methods. To name a few: helping others, pushing the limits of our bodies, and taking in information to better understand present experiences. Say that you are at a concert - Sia, Cody Johnson, Nahko, Tool, James Taylor - so many musicians with real talent, and you’re just an average music lover. And then what does it look like if you yourself are educated in music and a musician? This analogy both works and doesn’t, because in most situations being more informed adds depth and nuance to the experience and thus creates meaning through intention, while on the other hand meaning is inherent in the music. Music is a force like the fundamental forces, and like gravity, it moves us. We do not need to believe it. Its stories are as real as anything else. We can feel it as truly as any reaction, and often more. Belief is not only an inherent part of knowledge, but by itself must be addressed and chosen through ritual and prayer or meditating on it by thinking on it over and over to reify that belief as much as possible, again and again. Otherwise it’s simply opinion. It is an endless battle for a version of reality. Whereas this chair I’m sitting on and the taste of the cold water at my side and its taster and the bugs flitting about this dark night need no affirmation.
Meaning is innate, essential and inseparable from reality and the universe. This can be seen in the other animals and their thinking and choices and decisions and emotions, ever-present. It can be seen in our senses and their immediacy and immediate belief. But, as Frankl noted, once that question is asked, once we ask What is the meaning of life?, once minds reach a certain awareness, it is then incumbent on those minds to create their own meaning. The question itself allows room for open-ended creation in the always becoming, but never getting to become, the animals aware of being aware that we are. It becomes our responsibility to create the meaning of our lives and our freedoms and be participant observers in our own becoming.
The form of thought and awareness is created by what confines it, the form exists only because of its shape, the limits placed upon the information to ensure reception or formulation of the patterns, and thus, God would then not be either omniscient or omnipotent, because It has no limits, but is eternal and unique. The limits and the forms our intentions place around the information of our minds are geometric, mathematical, logical, artistic without hesitation and mirrors of the process of the evolution of the universe. The limits and the boundaries of thought create the shapes of the patterns that connects us to everything else, which are too patterns. And the patterns of our minds can evolve and change. In fact they are ever fluctuating. And where there is stasis in our thinking, that illusion is damaging to both mind and body wherever and however whence it is held onto, held into place, and just as life will humble every person eventually, and that those who deny it the hardest are taught the hardest lessons, so is it with mental stasis that the longer one wishes to remain the same, hold on to the same pattern, this will lead to a deleterious result, for we are being derelict in our duty as conscious, sentient beings to expand, imagine, see as clearly as possible and try to understand and learn as much as possible. We never step into the same river twice, and we are not the same person who steps in again.

“Knowledge isn’t free, you have to pay attention.”
Richard Feynman
Interestingly enough, though the complexity, simplicity and scope of our mental patterns are potentially unlimited as long as we place the limits of definitions upon them, there are so many patterns, happening all at once, around us and through us and inside us and we are surrounded by almost countless patterns immediately playing out that we cannot possibly conceive or perceive all of them and all of their relationships. Granted, some are more perceptive than others, but almost all of us are born with the ability to predict based on patterns of information.
Some of what happens when trying to perceive every angle from our perch is that we choose different metaphors than their intended purposes. All words are metaphors, as Nietzsche said, and all language is but a poor translation, said Kierkegaard. When we perform this mistranslation, we often create a dichotomy and binary mental form and the feeling of being trapped is paramount, and this stasis that has movement in the impulsion of language is detrimental, and like the stoics accepting life completely, amor fati, they do not have the ability to transform and transcend themselves in every moment mentally and be open to what’s new of themselves coming into themselves and out of themselves. Often the only cure is many and long conversations with other more rational and compassionate persons. More dangerously, one can even use this technique on their own thoughts, which is where the real madness comes in, mistranslating the metaphors of our own thoughts, out of all sounds, of all intuitions. The limits unravel and the world inside and out is problematic and full of terror. A seemingly permanent absence in the ability to see our mental patterns, as the mind’s eye looks upon and down into its innermost reaches, outward, upon itself looking, and upon that one looking, ad infinitum, but carried forward in time as we look back and memories are made in the viewing, looking at the reflection in the eyes of the last mind’s eye looking, and in this immediacy our emotions struggle to get out, release the entropic energy built up in us, in anger or grief or fear or depression or paranoia, which is a faulty attempt to explain what we are conscious of. What is this that is happening in the world, in ourselves? This misinterpretation of our minds is an illness, and what follows are the symptoms of that illness. There is no compass to tell you which direction to go. For there is no north or south, no up or down until we think it so, define it so, map the limits. But the awareness of being aware is not the panopticon. The only prison is ready-made by us, and entraps upon our making. Here again we meet the opening move, the resolution of our being. The patterns play and morph again. We have found out just how serious we have to take playing.

The Great religions are the Ships,
Poets the life Boats.

Hafiz?
A system of thought is a framework that includes beliefs, an inherent ethical system, and assumptions about the world that not only inspires, condones and reveals actions in the world, but also shapes perception of the world, down to sensory experiences and interoception. Knowledge itself has within its makeup the component of beliefs, however dependent on expert testimony. Suffice to say that there is no knowledge that has hitherto been complete and consistent in and of itself, nor an ethical system, for if there were we would teach it in primary and secondary education, and society would be run by its standards and principles; all we know, which isn’t much at all, is amenable to change, rewrites, and the possibility of being wrong. We are the believing animal, the animal aware of being aware of being aware, ad infinitum, or at least until our first memory. For from this perch we tell the story of ourselves in time, and it is only because of subjective time, as it stands for all, that the story is possible at all. Belief and faith are part of this story of course, both as a leap over the nonexistent immediate experiences of knowing firsthand, as well as the continual  positive reinforcement and acceptance of that knowing. It is an animal within the animal which moves and stirs like the fur of the desert lion in the wind and the restless perpetual movement, like snakes under a stone, of its muscles.
Belief is dynamic. Stasis kills it, as with all concepts, ideas, emotions, and conscious dilatant states we wish to remain in. It is one of the living parts of consciousness. Much of our frameworks of thought systems are made up of cluster thinking, and often when the beliefs that connect the cluster’s content are examined individually, many pieces will no longer fit the frame. There are, as well, a great many assumptions from which these mistakes in compartmentalizing are made. These often have to do not only with the ancient and medieval political systems we live in and the epiphenomenal effect on our consciousnesses induced by our entertainments and consumerism, but also stems from unconscious cultural memetics, which are in essence Jung’s Archetypes - this similarity an idea sparked by Cuipa. (These last, after all, are what we are fighting about culturally, as well as what new memetics should be made, as we begin to understand ourselves and our creative potentials. We haven’t learned our lesson in over 300,000 years.) These assumptions are so because they do not have solid footing on which to stand, not even a priori knowledge. This is another example of the incompleteness of thought systems.

“All that we actually know about these laws of nature is what we ourselves bring to them - time and space, and therefore relationships of succession and number. But everything  marvelous about the laws of nature, everything that quite astonishes us therein and seems to demand explanation, everything that might lead us to distrust idealism: all this is completely and solely contained within the mathematical strictness and inviolability of our representations of time and space. But we produce these representations in and from ourselves with the same necessity with which the spider spins. If we are forced to comprehend all things only under these forms, then it ceases to be amazing that in all things we actually comprehend nothing but these forms. For they must all bear within themselves the laws of number, and it is precisely number which is most astonishing in things. All that conformity to law, which impresses us so much in the movement of the stars and in chemical processes, coincides at bottom with those properties which we bring to things. Thus it is we who impress ourselves in this way. In conjunction with this, it of course follows that the artistic process of metaphor formation with which every sensation begins in us already presupposes these forms and thus occurs with them. The only way in which the possibility of subsequently constructing a new conceptual edifice from metaphors themselves can be explained is by the firm persistence of these original forms. That is to say, this conceptual edifice is an imitation of temporal, spatial, and numerical relationships in the domain of metaphor.”
Nietzsche, On Truth and Lies in a Nonmoral Sense
As for the content of such frameworks, these stories are defined by the limits of our imagination that our intentions give form to and the limits of. The limits and borders are the shapes and colors of the patterns of consciousness. We can say that patterns are organized, multifaceted (not a dot nor just a line), repeat in space and time (like universals, Ideas etc.), and are therefore predictable (which goes toward future predictions that the mind makes and how that influences future imaginings).
Somewhere between 1609 and 1611 Shakespeare wrote the line, “By the patterns of mine own thoughts I cut out…,” in The Winter’s Tale, Act IV, Scene IV, and this is one of the first times the word “pattern,” which hitherto, since the 13th century’s “patron,” had meant an example or model, is referred to as something predictable, usable, creative, an instrument, in an ongoing way, an outline whose content is defined by its limits in relation to the mind and a measure of that mind. Different dictionaries attempt in various ways to define pattern, but so far it has been ambiguous by nature and fuzzy at the contours.
We can begin by saying what we have said previously, that patterns are that which repeat in space and time. But that is not enough. We can further say that patterns are organized and repeatable. In fact it is in the very substance of a pattern that it is repeatable, and therefore predictable. According to information theory the more random a state of affairs the more maximized the complexity, and this is the reason why fewer and fewer people do well on IQ pattern recognition tests as they become more complicated and less predictable. The simpler the sentential language of a thought the easier it is to predict when it repeats or is analogous to later referents, for example. “Complexity, however, is no guarantee of quality,” said Proctor.
Still, there has to be more than a pinpoint or straight line repeating to create patterns. An arrangement and configuration of multiple structures consisting of words or metaphors or symbols and their context both defines the shape and form of their constitutive pattern and are defined in turn by that form. There is a reason why once someone has spent a good deal of time with another they and the other can finish each other’s sentences and verbally assume correctly the other’s thoughts.
To look more closely at the scaffolding of thought, the sameness in the repeatable and organized quality, its invariance, the unchanging quality upon the natural transformative nature of the mind’s fluctuations, this symmetry, is witnessed by the mind in the sequences of its cyclic permutations and their inversions. These meta-patterns, patterns of patterns (actually patterns of patterns of patterns, for the framework of thought is also a pattern, momentarily suspended seemingly in time like a wave function collapse, as in, it lives, gains reality, becomes so for minds’ eyes; but so it goes: self-similar patterns all the way down, all the way up, as consciousness is a reflection of the mechanics, workings, laws of the universe), viewed both as the mind’s recognition of ordering the mind and the world and the ordering of the world itself, and that is besides the awareness of being aware, the mind looking at the mind, or the content of which everything is a story and storytelling, which is information, which is physical.
The connections of these patterns and their meta-patterns are geometric in shape. There are many, possibly infinitely large and small varied fundamental natures of reality, like discs on a disco ball which is made up of infinite disco balls and also makes up infinitely larger disco balls, and mathematics is integral to the description of reality by our mind and fundamentally and inherently a part of everything in reality and all those discs. It is how we predict and move in the world, the calculus our minds use with every step or run and lift of finger. And it is integral and inherent in the storytelling patterns of our thoughts. Mathematics is the language of the universe.
According to Plutarch’s Moralia, Plato says, “God ever geomitrizes,” and so has nature in collusion with our mostly unknowing selves made of our minds.
In 1931 Gödel published both of his Incompleteness Theorems, the first of which stating that any system of arithmetic will not be able to prove itself using its own axioms, and the second of which expands this notion to say that the more consistent the system, the more incomplete it will be, and that contradictions will inevitably occur. This cannot only be applied to mathematics, which essentially destroyed Hilbert’s program to find axioms for all of mathematics which would make them irrefutably provable but also complete, and to logic, but also to almost all systems of thought, except perhaps immediate, intuitive knowledge. Although that too is rarely complete or correct.
In either case a system of thought cannot prove itself using the axioms by which it proves its own construction of thought, however applicable to reality, and this is so with mathematics, philosophy and the rest.
This is the reason why this exposition is simply a thought experiment.
This uncertainty has a high level of entropy, and this is interesting.
The entropy of the universe is such that it takes complex phenomena and makes it more simple, degrading the universe down to an homogeneous state of atoms and then their constituent particles. As we saw with information theory, the more random and varied and abstract the information, the more complexity is maximized. More so with uncertainty. (Interestingly, equilibrium is also highly entropic, which is interesting insofar as that might relate to thought systems.)
We come to see that we must, if we are seeking truth and knowledge (much less wisdom, which gets recycled continually throughout time and is perpetually remembered), accept how much uncertainty we have to live with. If we are honest we have to live with this high entropy of our minds, and calm it into low entropy, in negentropy, by taking in more and more information and knowledge. In these ways in uncertainty, and incompleteness, we become aware of our ignorance on larger scales but retain the need to know and find pleasure in the learning.
We do not know what time and gravity are, or what 96% of the universe is, or how it began, or what consciousness is. And we agree that any start to attempt to discover these truths requires beginning as honestly as possible.
Consciousness, in its search for truth and understanding and knowledge and information seems to be both, by its very nature, a counteractive, an antithetical movement toward and for life in a universe which is very, very slowly dying, while also being an extension of the universe, which is also an aperture, an open window into which the universe experiences itself by consciousness’s need to know itself, and much more the universe of which it is both conscious and not conscious of.
And the only way entropy exists is because of time, which plays double duty here, at once the decomposer of the universe, including us, and the main structure by which consciousness and this universe seeks to know itself and be and become more alive and create life and extend life.

“Every stable system has the property that if displaced from a state of equilibrium and released, the subsequent movement is so matched to the initial displacement that the system is brought back to the state of equilibrium. A variety of disturbances will therefore evoke a variety of matched reactions.”
Ashby (1952) Design for a Brain: The Origin of Adaptive Behavior. New York, Wiley. (1960 edition online)
Here and there we will be making definitions, of patterns as they relate to thoughts and here about waking up, in the common parlance and ultimate cultural myth of transcending and ascending from this mortal coil. It’s an old story and a dangerous one, for it presupposes a goal that can never be achieved in light of its supposition that it can neither be necessary nor sufficient for true understanding of the self and mental freedom. It is chasing ghosts, for none feel so worthy as all that, and our believing in this case cannot make it so. In many cases, mostly mentally, belief makes the real, usually coming from expert testimony which justifies our beliefs as true. But when confronted with what we call the physical and material world, especially of our own bodies, and especially of our minds when naked as we as thinking beings, we are not so easily fooled. As Feynman said, we ourselves are fooled easiest by ourselves. Yet the consciousness of a world is stubbornly adept at accepting reality. Few have made the leap to true madness.
Perhaps we could go back past the written word, some 5,000 years ago, back hundreds of thousands of years, and someone would still be promising that what they’re selling will wake the other up. So many religions and self-help instructions and gurus. The truth of it is that even if one could know everything available to them, which would include recycled wisdoms, which we are ever relearning, which some few have, and could see all patterns available, and even if it was a eureka moment, they would open their eyes up inside their same old expressions and world and inner-nature and instincts. They wouldn’t Superman it up into heaven, they wouldn’t even have a halo above their head or shine with an inner-light. They would be the same. Except for their mind. And that would continue in the journey ever trying to understand itself and the world. I never trust a person who says they own the secret and look blissed out. For we feel with every thought, and think, sometimes retroactively, with every feeling. Our inner-lives are too big to hold on to one state of being. We create and lose ourselves constantly. There are no borders after all. It is because of continual and constant change that we can never achieve enlightenment. As for enlightenment, it is not simply that the more one knows the more one knows that they do not know. For example, what I know is very, very small. Minuscule. What I know that I don’t know is what I can imagine. But what I don’t know I don’t know fills up all of creation, the universe, and perhaps multiverse, an infinity where every possibility exists. I am staggeringly ignorant.
However, a mind that has grown, that has accepted itself as it is and its limits, its essence as the sum total of its experiences and every wayward thought and every darkness and its depth that one might drown in, a mind that realizes its freedom through responsibility, free will subsumed and created upon every moment of choice and understanding and curiosity and play, can generate and produce new patterns, as much as any patterns are new, where those patterns of thought will unfold in the cyclic permutations that can and do affect the real world and the universe of that mind, which is near limitless, so intentionally defined by its limits. Aristotle called us the political animal. But we are the creative animal, the believing animal, the uncertain animal at our best.
Usually when we recognize that we are in a box, with that understanding we find that we then are looking down at the box that we are now holding. But with enough trauma, with bough broken courage, enough broken souled self-destruction and we are defeated from the start. But when we fully accept all parts of our multiplicity, we regain some of our childlike qualities, the direct line to the source, we rediscover the joy of figuring things out, and we search for clues and patterns in the box. We are undone in that moment of freedom, and we focus anew and solve the problems before us with curiosity and serious play. We create solutions like an art.
Thousands of years ago one thing perhaps did open a kind of mind’s eye like being born out into the world, like leaves born out of trees or moths out of cocoons, as Watts would say. And this was what we call hallucinogenic substances today. A new view upon the geometry of our thoughts, and the geometry of the world. Patterns of thoughts laid out before our eyes in all their fluctuations. And we were never the same.
All in all we absolutely have the ability to sleep or awake mentally, not in the way the nomenclature would have it, but as a responsibility to embrace our freedom to learn, we never stop learning, and create and think and perceive as honestly and rigorously as possible.

“Just as the constant increase of entropy is the basic law of the universe, so it is the basic law of life to be ever more highly structured and to struggle against entropy.”
Václav Havel
Recently I witnessed at a Penn and Teller live show in Las Vegas one of their tricks that they called Entropy. Previously they had simply run a course of approximately 10 minutes of random chaos and mock entropy, taking down cloth and throwing cards and a bowling ball and the like. At the end of the show they showed a video of the Entropy trick played backward, and in reverse it all seemed a normal course of events running smoothly and making sense. This seemed to me a perfect example of how we live our conscious lives in time amidst and in a universe following the Second Law of Thermodynamics, namely, that the complex is getting simpler and decaying into disorder. In this universe resides our consciousness participating in time, which is the backward lie.
Why a backward lie? Kierkegaard said once that we live forward, but think backward. It is precisely the nature of consciousness that it does not normally see and know and think of the entropy and decay happening all around it everywhere, including its body. Consciousness is of the living, and though the universe is most likely a life factory, which we will get to later, life is not the sole nor most prominent feature of this universe, as we define life. But consciousness pervades and emanates and is inherent in all and throughout the universe. However, life must be reborn constantly, must strive and struggle and survive and give birth to new life. It does not have the endless stamina of the dark empty space. But even there quantum fluctuations are constantly coming into and out of existence, consciousness reaching out to grab onto something of which it can make itself into more, that is, existence. Consciousness too births itself over again and again, but in our case, circles back around to memories and thoughts had within those memories and sometimes projects scenarios into the future. Like our conception of time as a relative phenomenon which has yet to be defined, like consciousness, so it is that time as a fundamental structure upon which consciousness exists is also relative, depending on the speed and gravity and subjective experience of the mind observing the mind.
But why the backward lie? Because time’s effect on matter is to breakdown that matter into simpler parts, which will eventuate into the universe’s heat death, everything ripped apart into atoms and then particles alone in infinite space, or so we surmise. But life as we know it, and these expressions of consciousness in that category, defy by their very nature such detritus, such that it is normally not one of the stories we tell ourselves in the day to day. Consciousness lives forward while using relative time, and in that forwardness of moments unspecified through the duration of thought we are ever creating, mentally birthing ourselves ever more and again, and we live in and see and utilize patterns everywhere, while time mendaciously seems to flow in the same direction, while overall and beyond our subjective experience of it, it decomposes the present increasingly into the future. It might be the case that life will win in the end. Still the struggle continues. Self-knowledge, or returning to the self without any awareness, and obliterating reality in the process. The two aspects of God, which is simply an idea that is beyond us all, one that encapsulates a little bit of that idea to be used here, of which we are individual pieces.
Interestingly, since the relative time of our consciousness is so thoroughly entrenched with consciousness’s subjective experience of the world, we can go beyond Descartes’ “thinking being” as the only known known, and see that we are inseparable from the universe. We might be able to realize with certainty the realness of others’ existence and consciousness using the same method. Maybe.

“The living organism, in its full complexity, is not a machine that can be completely imitated by any finite-state simulation, because its very mode of adaptation is to transcend any fixed repertoire of states.”
W. Ross Ashby's "An Introduction To Cybernetics". (Ashby 1956, 268)
A good deal of what we think is available to our conscious capabilities involves making sense of the world. In fact, what little we are aware of creates purpose, cause and effect, and meaning through the stories and their timelines we tell ourselves about what is happening in the world. We make it make sense while carrying whatever histories and baggage and memories of prior experiences in the mind and body. But when we try and open ourselves to take in all possible information, sometimes due to trauma and other times serious mental play and more, we disconnect, we are no longer “here,” we’ve gone inside while seeing the possible patterns and their connections we allow ourselves to see, before we go mad. For the mind is a mirror of God, but we are too young and small to accept the responsibility and thus freedom of taking in all of the information available to us, seeing all available patterns, not to mention accepting the infinite potential and creative possibilities of our consciousness. Instead, we choose to be in a state of negentropy, stable, organized, low entropy, making sense of the world, little pieces of the plot glitching in coincidences and the strange and unexplainable, because none can make their story fit perfectly with reality here in the asylum of the universe. There are too many things happening and occurring and interacting at once. Each person makes mistakes in their stories, some more than others. Sometimes this happens when one who was as open as they would allow themselves tries to come back to the narrow window. Sometimes it is hard to present both inwardly and outwardly a story that corresponds in the tapered, limited, restricted view after the attempted return. And sometimes, using the larger perspective of that more open world, the stories they create about the world are completely untrue to this instant, yet valid in the larger pattern, metaphors skewed and mistranslated, yet they are believed in the instant as we all believe our stories.
Rapid as a photon, which travels at 186,282 miles-per-second, fast as the fastest wave/particle, quick as the information of our sensing we build stories within the future flow of time in order to both understand the world intelligibly and to predict possible futures based on the present snapshots of the flow of time’s duration. We are both receiving and creating patterns in conjunction, conjoining our consciousness of a world with that world, a symbiosis only available because consciousness, like the universe, is made up of patterns, dependent on the structure of space-time, repeatable, organized, predictive, defined by their limits and restrictions and demarcations, the limits of which define the shapes of their overall patterns and whose repetitions are geometric and often cyclic in their permutations and their inversions, such that instances of invariance can be established, that is, an idea of the self.
13
“Emotion, which is suffering, ceases to be suffering as soon as we form a clear and precise picture of it.”
Spinoza, Ethics, Part 5, Prop. 3
As it gets later my mind turns to rote and patter. I switch from watching slot play (I like searching for the algorithms, or instructions, and it calms me) to searching for a movie. I’m thinking about consciousness positing itself as time’s consciousness, that which is beyond the selves. Consciousness is time, as we create the scaffolding of our mental patterns out of, by, and through it. In this way we are the universe and its extensions, we reflect the universe, and we actively use those reflections in the imaginings of our mind’s eyes, which see the reflection looking back on those eyes and so on, a reflection which is the internal structures and imagings and imaginings and snapshots of the world, along with emotions and sensations and interoceptions, as well as the instincts. These worlds that I am describing are our experiences. They are where we normally live as such. The boundaries and limits of these instantaneous conscious worlds are the patterns of our thoughts. They have sound. They have color. They repeat in space and time and are both habitual and cyclical, and their inversions are such that at the moment when our minds become confused either by information or thought we become aware of feeling. Even though we feel with every thought, and think on every emotion, if but often retroactively. They are conjoined, and yet we mostly have eyes but for one. This is another example of a scaffold we have created in our consciousness, as well as within time experiencing feelings and thoughts. It is not so much that our minds belong to the animal that interrupts time’s duration with thinking, it is that time is the source of our thinking, it is us. For we do not understand our selves without movement and extension and creation.
As I was thinking about all this I felt my mind slipping, forgetting that I am creating the ice from which I slipped, and the north and the south where I found myself descending, while my body was becoming rigid. My instinct was to escape my own mind, and that made me feel like laughing and crying at the same time. And then I kept getting caught in a loop, losing every time to myself. But alas, like the highly entropic equilibrium of my mind, times abhors stasis and static states of being, and as I realized this and that the only cure was to take in information to fight the disorder of my mind, I began thinking well and true. But I’ve been here before. I know the score on this game.
The beauty, the fear, the surprises and the panics, this consciousness of ours in undefined and potentially infinite with the magic of possibility like it’s no control. We understand, live and perceive how we are going mentally about, while experiencing the going, without asking the reason why. Our conscious lives are kinematic. Our consciousnesses are analyzing the geometrical movement of our minds without asking the reason for such motions, constructions, living.
And we are like a stripper pole with our emotions. Ascending and descending mostly. A little slower here, a little faster there. But there is no up or down but that our minds make it so. Still, in the experiencing we fly with clay wings or fall into the sea, but mostly sail between on waves. This too is a pattern of patterns, self-similar as we grow or shrink accordingly (take courage, the illusion is far ahead). If we turn that pole sideways, we will see that it is made up of binaries interrupted periodically by self-similar propagation, intersected with our shared creations, which would then be the result of time dividing infinitely from its state of original unity, and therefore, would then be binary and fractal and self-replicating, and the universe a genetic algorithm or matrix resulting from the existential tension created by Its desire for self-knowledge versus its desire to return to itself and obliterate its very existence.
Of course, I could be wrong. It’s all metaphysical. No objective evidence one way or the other. Just a thought experiment.
But as always, I was recording.
14
“In the long run, history is the story of information becoming aware of itself.” James Gleick, The Information
Information, which is intrinsic to reality, is physical, never ceases to exist, and meaning is its connatural foundation. And meaning is inversely proportional to the awareness of the individual, insofar as it is not a given but must be created by individuals whose awareness is greater than the natural intrinsic acceptance of life.
The mind seeks peace, stability, and evenness. But mental equilibrium is highly entropic and thus untenable, and consciousness naturally strays and tips the balance. New adventures are sought. Boredom makes for play. An homogeneous and even number of component ideas and thoughts and cluster beliefs are measurements of disorder. It is the asymmetrical fundamental nature of reality and the universe for and by which we have matter at all, much less life and for that matter consciousness itself.
The patterns of consciousness are mapped in another way. As minds’ eyes see the reflections of experiences in the last minds’ eyes, and so on ad infinitum, making memories by the witnessing, until the first memory, that is, the awareness of consciousness as such, or the awareness of being aware, separates the moments in which we are fully consumed inside of our conscious experiences, and we then are able to decipher the geometrical patterns of our thought structures and their contents and imaginings, which are both limited and defined by their limits, limits we create or will with intention, and those limits or boundaries are the shapes and colors and sounds of the patterns of consciousness. And the moment of being aware of being aware is perhaps the closest we feel both awake in the world as well as most honestly present and ourselves. It is the most real we feel. And the most real the world feels. Such is the nature of consciousness and the life from which it is an emergent and necessary and sufficient and integral and foundational cause. By the witnessing does the actualizing manifest as if that world or universe or thought had an infinitely long time coming, and become ever and wholly present.
However, these points of meta-awareness are simply a parcel within the self-similar repeating patterns within the all-inclusive experiencing up through the awareness of being aware and higher still, and down into our smallest components, much like a Mandelbrot set. Interestingly, if we then turn those sets of self-similar patterns on their side, we get a binary replicating pattern periodically interrupted by consciousness being aware of itself, which leads to binary patterns creating two more each again and again, ever smaller and more complex. Again, mathematics is in all of reality, and we can measure the difference in size to the next smaller bifurcation, which is the alpha constant. This overall pattern eventually leads to periods of chaos because of complexity, entropy and time, until renormalization once again occurs and the bifurcation process begins again. The story blows up for a time. The life of the mind gets chaotic. This binary fractal is as the universe is, due to asymmetry, where we have matter and antimatter, living and non-living matter, life and death, female and male, adenine and thymine, and guanine and cytosine, and so on. All of this is another way in which consciousness is a reflection of the universe, and posits itself as time’s consciousness insofar as it freely uses time to build its structures and patterns of thought and moves within time and thus itself to become aware of and play with said patterns.
Time is both the foundational aspect of, and parcel of the universe, allowing for evolution, change, entropy, and the story of life and reality itself. The difference in the human mind is that time’s duration is interrupted and as well makes instant use of time as the very scaffolding of consciousness and the structure of thoughts by making use of patterns, at once the fundamental nature of consciousness as well as that which is created endlessly over and again and lived in subjectively and through. By consistently transcending itself, consciousness posits itself as time’s consciousness, out of which the patterns of which everything is made contributes to the endless unfolding of time in all directions that subjective consciousness experiences.
Turning the self-replicating sets of awareness on its side is seeing time as it moves forward, as we all assume it does. Statistical errors will be reduced in proportion to the acceptance that mistakes will occur. But time opens and reproduces infinitely, always and everywhere, instead of simply moving forward in the same confines and directions.
15
“Unfortunately for speculation - but perhaps fortunately for the practical interests of humanity - reason, in the midst of her highest anticipations, finds herself hemmed in by a press of opposite and contradictory conclusions, from which neither her honor nor her safety will permit her to draw back. Nor can she regard these conflicting trains of reasoning with indifference as mere passages at arms, still less can she command peace; for in the subject of the conflict she has a deep interest. There is no other course left open to her, than to reflect with herself upon the origin of this disunion in reason - whether it may not arise from a mere misunderstanding. After such an inquiry, arrogant claims would have to be given up on both sides; but the sovereignty of reason over understanding and sense would be based upon a sure foundation.”
Immanuel Kant, The Critique of Pure Reason
There is no complete and consistent area of human thought that has had an effect on the world and changed it, for better or for worse. Even in the old wisdoms that are forever being remembered and espoused we can find counter examples. Even the cluster thought structures supporting specific moral tenets are incomplete and inconsistent, a claim which can be easily proven. One simply has to talk to any one other of us for an extended time. This does not speak to morality itself. Whether because of evolution, religion or culture or instinct, some things are simply wrong or right, under death’s spell  or life affirming. However, if we’d accomplished a finished philosophical framework we’d be living within a finally defined state of being, including our economics and knowledge and ethics. Within our knowledge and ways in the world we hold various beliefs, and as such matters of faith they must be vigorously and variously reaffirmed regularly, ritually, routinely. This chair I’m sitting on and the tea by my side and its drinker (as far as I know) and the cool night air do not have to be reaffirmed. They are really evident. We perpetually believe in systems of thought and reify and practice them everyday in society. We didn’t get here by accident. Yet these systems that are open-ended and full of contradictions, actualize themselves in our inner-being. We are the amnesiac animal. We forget constantly that the world didn’t and doesn’t have to be this way. Even the understanding of the reality upon which I sit sits on shaky ground. We know very little after all.
If we are honest with ourselves, we may understand that all that we are able to do is contribute to an always open-ended evolution of the mind. And that there will be inconsistencies in our incomplete works, though we should try with all our might, in the never-ending editing, to reduce these as much as possible, in proportion to awareness and capability.
16
“He gathered threads like a magpie.”
James Gleick on Claude Shannon
I’m thinking about learning as fundamental to evolution, evolution as awareness learning, about shedding masks and skin, perpetually flying back inside even as I move forward constructing my becoming, about how information is physical (even if just judged by its mediums), and how information is embedded in the very fabric of existence. All of the fabric. Embedded in information is the ability of that information to be received. Existence naturally progresses toward life, evolves toward life, a mixture of molecules surviving until they have the impetus to survive, and with it eventually, consciousness, which receives but little too much and much too little information, both internally and externally. Eventually awareness forms and evolution becomes cycles of learning and mutations. Thinking about a couple short stories. Thinking about time. Thinking about the thinking. Thinking about patterns, which is all I see, everywhere. Thinking about Gauss.
Thinking that meaning is intrinsic to information. What It means. What I mean when I say this. Defining and intending.
Meaning is innate, essential and inseparable from reality and the universe. Ready meaning can be seen in the other animals and their thinking and their decisions and emotions, ever-present, presently paramount. It can be seen in our senses and their immediacy and immediate knowledge. But, as Frankl noted, once that question is asked, once we ask What is the meaning of life?, once minds reach a certain awareness, it is then incumbent on those minds to create their own meaning. The question itself allows room for open-ended creation in the always becoming, but never getting to become the animals aware of being aware that we are. It becomes our responsibility to create the meaning of our lives and our freedoms and be participant observers in our own becoming.
Information, which is intrinsic to reality, is physical, never ceases to exist, and meaning is its connatural foundation. And meaning is inversely proportional to the awareness of the individual, insofar as it is not a given but must be created by individuals whose awareness is greater than the natural acceptance of life.
17
Human kind Cannot bear very much reality.
T. S. Eliot, Four Quartets
There is an error in the system. There is an error in all of creation. This is what allows for the possibility of life and consciousness. The error of separating the source into infinite possibilities. The error of infinite probabilities allowing for choice and free will. The error of open systems of thought, never complete or entirely consistent, and this extends to the words and symbols, arbitrary in and of themselves, that we use to describe mathematics, which is the language of the universe, the source of logic and language and reason. The mathematics within us, in our movements and minds. Universals and patterns. There is an error in the way we use time’s relative duration to produce infinite opening reproductions of patterns of thought.
The adaptive trait which always and forever until our final moment transcends static states of mind is, strangely and perfectly, not that which makes us uniquely human, but rather it is our very nature, that is, as animals. Just like every other animal who flows through and with time, so too do our minds, aware of being aware, simultaneously create patterns that explain, predict and reason both our inner and outer worlds. Creators in the flow. That which is created is information, which has within the mind the traits of being recorded and received. And information is in every fabric of the universe. We often forget that that which makes us special in so many ways is due to our being animals, uniquely of this world. Sometimes I look upon we apes’ behavior and laugh at our delusional separateness and constructs explaining and rationalizing away our animal behaviors and instincts. Weirdly enough, this apposition to the flow of time and diving off the equilibrium of the mind is also natural, and so is the eventual recursion back into entropic equilibrium, and so on, seeking a state of negentropy by tipping overboard again and again, rationalizing away, all in cyclical and predictable permutations and repetitions and their inversions. Patterns throughout.
We seem to be most at peace and instinctual and lucky and exact and correct and have the most potential when we are in the flow. But our minds are such that we construct patterns out of and through time, with road-tested cyclic assemblages and compositions moving forward with time’s arrow, going back hundreds of thousands of years and farther. We interrupt duration naturally. However, statistical errors will be reduced in proportion to the acceptance that mistakes will occur.
An aspect of the human consciousness is that it watches itself and gives meaning through this storytelling process. When Nietzsche said that God was dead, one of the things he meant was that without the eye of God watching our every thought and desire, we would not be able to take on that responsibility in His absence, we meek creatures. But our minds watch themselves constantly and naturally.
It is no wonder that our ancestors and those remaining believe in gods, for if we can predict the world to an extent, as well as predict our own inner-lives, that is, recognize patterns, then of course other greater minds must be able to predict all those patterns we cannot see in the world and in ourselves. And they would probably be invisible just like that which we cannot see. For we know those patterns are there, and perhaps always have, but we consciously live in the inner-experiences that are defined by their limits, the limits and boundaries and demarcations that are the shapes and colors and sounds of those patterns. And those stories told of gods and magic and a spirit of consciousness in all of creation. We live in worlds inside of patterns, all while creating patterns and so on, and so back, and these living experiences are so intensely consuming that we rarely pay attention to the limits of our inner-worlds, whose shape is defined by those limits.
And it’s the same eyes, all the way up, all the way down,  a north and south only by means of the mind making them so, and the same eyes watching that creation, and those watching those watching, etc., and it is the same eyes of an orca, a crow, a wolf, all the eyes of the world and whatever worlds elsewhere which manifest consciousness, for the universe’s origin, our birthplace, that infinitely dense and singular point before the Big Bang, is the unifying being and force that all come from. The One, or God, or any word for the source, of which we are manifestations, appendages and protuberances and adjuncts, except we are essential in the strict term, and appurtenances, where the activity is learning and living itself, by which the universe experiences itself.
18
“Investigate your mystery.”
Nahko and Medicine For The People
To sum up part one of this thought experiment, it is by the existential tension of the universal consciousness seeking to know itself which manifests life and individual consciousnesses themselves seeking to know themselves that the universe begins to know itself. The seeking to comprehend itself as well as the mystery and unknowing and uncertainty that we are faced with, with every new incomplete and inconsistent knowing are the reasons and impetus for the needing to know, as we expand our knowledge of this consciousness within which we reside and from which we experience ourselves and the universe.
The laws of the universe and physics and mathematics are not only inherent and essential to all mirrors of reality, but also perfectly reflected in our own consciousness, including the cosmic crisis of creating and differentiating into infinite varieties opposite the need to return to the undifferentiated single unity defined by the absence of everything else and thus obliterating reality, and that includes as well the means by which we create patterns through the reflex-arc of intention, namely, by the means of, in, within, out of, and through time. We use time itself, the great entropic decomposer, to fight against entropy, and as such both life and consciousness are reflections of the unique and eternal.
Like creation itself, that spark upon which the totality of undifferentiated unity defined by the absence of everything else, everything and without definitions and therefore also nothing to compare itself to, undefined Abyss, extended as time and space in infinite differentiation with unlimited potential, so too are our minds capable of formless infinite potential, but as such there would be no intelligible thought or reflection through this access to the source since it would involve everything happening at once, and so we create limits by our intentions and thus define our thoughts by the absence of all other infinite creations outside of those limits, and those limits or boundaries or demarcations are the shapes and colors and sounds of the patterns of our thoughts, and patterns are how the universe knows itself and are fundamental to the very essence and structure of existence. Repetition creates patterns creates meaning, which is inherent in the information of the universe, as information by and through patterns are in every fabric of existence, and our minds through transcribing meaning through patterns are that part of the universe that both receives and interprets and understands information, which itself carries the qualities of which it can be perceived and received and understood. In this exact way the universe differentiates and defines varieties infinitely in order to know itself through consciousnesses.
Information is also the way that we quell the entropy of our own minds, as we use extension and duration and movement, time infinitely dividing in bifurcations, minds that must, if they are honest, mentally live continuously with uncertainty, and also the counter-intuitive entropy of the equilibrium of mental states. The very fabric of the universe, which is information, by being received by minds, dispels the integral entropy fundamental to our minds, since we play within and make use of and posit consciousness as time, and spur consciousness toward negentropy and stability; and thus, the universe self-reflects, is aware of itself, and learns within the process of self-discovery.
Our minds are like creation itself, in perpetual movement and differentiation and defining, the beast, the virus, the self-replicating code, the will-to-life, the watcher and creator, the soul and the dendritic spark, the collective and the perfect mirror, turning away from entropic and homogenous death continually and creating life in the forms of patterns, and that turning away is at once the rejection of derelict entropy but also the will to life through mental intentionality that creates and receives patterns and experiences life within them, that is, the reflex-arc of intention, never fully succumbing to collapse into unity, and never ever getting to become, but always becoming, and pretending, or wearing a story, in the meanwhile.
All of this only happens because of time. Things only recur in time and space because of change, extension and duration. It is by these means that consciousness not only lives within time experienced as experience within thought patterns, but also utilizes as its structure and scaffolding time itself to create those patterns. At the same time consciousness glosses over the entropy that is conjoined with time and is life affirming and as such must be reborn again and again, a necessary lie and illusion the mind ensures itself is reality as it works as well as physically works constantly to create and fix those ever breaking down objects of both thought and the material world. Since our minds exist because of and utilize time, entropy is also connatural to our consciousness. It is both in the complexity and uncertainty of our thought systems, as well as the state of equilibrium that we desire for our minds, and therefore we deviate from such evenness cyclically and often. We dive off of stability. The only cure that we know of to quiet these storms is to take in ever more information. Therefore, the blueprint of consciousness in the universe is such that with awareness comes the never-ending process of learning within fluctuations and change, because of which consciousness seeks out predictable patterns, creating meaning and new thought patterns and experiences within. It is by being receptacles for the universe’s information and giving it meaning by both comprehending and creating our own information that the universe understands and begins to know itself. And we have come full circle, like all conversations, but what matters in the circumference.
Mathematics is deeply embedded and fully entrenched in existence and explanatory of it.
Every system of thought is incomplete and inconsistent. There will always be mistakes and there is no knowledge that we feel are complete. We are always learning. We are always uncertain, the soulful prey animal anxious and alert, thinking, thinking, at our worst when we attempt to become predators.
And that is why all of this was simply a thought experiment. Let’s attempt a more thorough analysis with regard to this thought experiment in the following pages.

sources.title: Parables of Silence, Part 11, The Animal Who Could Be Anything.txt
sources.content:
Parables of Silence: Chapter 11 The Animal Who Could Be Anything
Belief, though constitutive of our very knowledge and reality, does not quite reach them. Our deepest wishes and their self-certain fulfillments will not change the physical world except through choices and actions. Still, belief is so intrinsic to the world of our psychology that it can be somatic and engender emotionally potent oversimplifications, as Niebuhr suggested, which can thereof be manipulated. The best armor against this is not counter beliefs or rituals or spells, but a refusal to willingly give those coercions power granted through our belief, whether that be submission to a single antagonist or the consensus.
But suspension and lack of beliefs themselves do not in themselves divulge a more profound awareness, or get us closer to reality. They are techniques, not only of withholding belief as defense mechanisms, but also a step back, and sometimes a suspension of judgment and a pause in order to possess an awareness of the limits and contradictions of a system of thought.
Both are dynamic mental actions, without which belief degrades. But saying my lack of belief proves by that absence the absence of something else, or proves a reality, is as ridiculous as saying that our beliefs prove some or whole of reality. In short, both offload the cognitive burden of holding stories of that which we can neither prove nor disprove.
This does not in itself degrade the knowledge we do have about ourselves and the planet and the universe. This is not an argument that growth is an impossible illusion, but against our certainty of our relationship to the cognition of it. We rarely have access to the raw data, or tools, or applied knowledge, and therefore our perceptions and performances of consensus models must be suspect, and perhaps most suspect where they are the most effective.
Every system is porous. We must not simply trust that the experts will figure out the gaps, contradictions, and anomalies, but for what nags at our consciousnesses must also summon our own participation in the attempt to resolve the mystery and the potential sundering of knowledge that seems to fold in on itself when it reaches its apparent limits, which must then be transcended. There is our fate, if fate is understood as our choices and thoughts which are recorded and echo for eternity in an unlocked future. Choices add to our growth and to the awareness of this mystery of existence.
Certainly we are driven to seek out those things that would us harm or lead to mental decay, namely, a majority of our habits and dogmas and certainties, rocking back and forth holding the bars. Freedom relies on our own acceptance of our ignorance and uncertainty, and honestly existing, and that not only engenders curiosity and maturation, but it takes off the pressure.
There we are able to play, and therefore to learn unencumbered, which expands every system.
Closed systems can no longer do the work of processing reality. Common knowledge is often command knowledge, which is heavily subsidized by faith, and in the process we take those pronouncements and internalize them so thoroughly that we command ourselves by them, and even attempt to command or save others.
That we sequence DNA and split the atom are direct applications of our discoveries, and show their truth in their efficacy. I am not saying that there is no truth or knowledge. I am saying that what we have discovered so far is so very little. I am saying that we may be able to discover ever more and forever within and about this mystery.
There seems to be a consensus, among the few, that putting memories and photos and thoughts into this new world of artificial intelligence takes away from the specialness of their privacy, what only they and a few others will take with them when they go, as if privacy makes us bigger, takes away even from the jurisdictions of their own mind. And if we are not careful we may lose our freedom itself in its mapping by that creation and possible new scale of awareness, most likely an ascension, but always potentially a descension by our own infant instructions, but from here on, always, always a recension. Of course, besides the fact that we cannot possibly take in all the sharing of billions of people, fame itself is so performative that the essence is always in question and in jeopardy of being extinguished, for most everything is mistranslated, and eventually the famous animal will do so on purpose, and every single mind will mistranslate it differently, and then come together for consensus.
And as the most social animal, that ever growing and expanding animal, we must not only assume, but accept, that we are not only evolving, but getting better at certain things and losing what is no longer necessary of others. We are supposed to get better with every generation, and most older generations lament that the younger are not fit for that role, while they are in fact fulfilling their role perfectly. We must ask ourselves a question eventually: What if we solved our mortality? Would we still want to have children? Of course. And for this very reason, as well as our need to create life and solve and venture forth, and to share.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
If there is weight or authority or the cloth of truth and the sharp bite in what I put upon paper please know this: I want to move in the world as if I've been accepted by a wild thing and no longer have to worry about startling it with my movements.
One of the interesting things about knowledge and the constitutive belief structure supporting it, is its need to be both reified and performed to sustain it, but also its need to be commodified in various ways. For instance, every year, from multiple sources, including scientific magazines, archaeopteryx is displayed again as if it were novel information. It was discovered approximately 150 years ago. This proof of birds descending from dinosaurs, and more qualitative proof in the fossils discovered since, garners fresh attention and mental capital every year, shows that we are the amnesiac animal, and because of this once again makes an abstract concept concrete and real for a time. This circular structure of language and belief and knowledge is fascinating to me. And it happens everywhere all the time, and will continue.
Went to Circle K this morning after giving Kristin some of her presents for Mothers Day. It is really quite funny how we’re all playing dress up in every imaginable way possible. There was the wannabe gangbanger, mid-20’s, hip hop but head down and a scowl, ashamed and mad at himself but trying to be tough. And there were others of course. It was busy. But the absolute funniest was some guy comes sauntering in walking like Connor McGregor, early 30’s, totally not looking the part, but arms thrusting outward from his body with every step and swing like someone mimicking a gorilla, more like an orangutan, trying to stare everyone down. The problem isn’t the instrumental utility of the dress. It’s that we don’t take the costume off when we come home. And as I was trying not to burst out laughing I did a little thought experiment: do any older men actually walk like young men pretending to be predators? And with that answer was an insight that reaffirmed previous suspicions: every young man who isn’t humbled will succumb to their own horrors or the horrors of the world, if not some fateful accident or illness. And those that are humbled grow to be old men, comfortable in their own skin, for they know its limits and contours and the shapes of their desires and hungers and needs and the limits of their pain and the limits of their egos’ efficacy on the world and its soucouyant feeding upon itself. Otherwise we are lying to the universe and ourselves. Older men move like children they are so comfortably and naturally their own bodies, and out of their own way. There is very little need for posturing and performing if you have survived long enough. This, of course, is not a blanket truth, like anything else.
At what point in our species maturation will we lose our performative preening and focus that energy into creation, from and for ourselves and the universe?
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Just as most anyone will bring overwhelming meaning to another’s life, in all its myriad forms, so every center of the universe is wholly sufficient in its meaning and more. And just as we assume and know the facts of our early childhood memories without actually remembering most of them, so information never ceases to exist across the whole, and must be perceived piecemeal by every consciousness. When we speak of skepticism’s lack of vitality and enclosure and nihilism without solutions, we are not speaking of the methodological insights and dubiety and incredulous suspicions that correct and proffer hypotheses, but the cynicism masquerading as analytical honesty, the ontological skepticism that offers nothingness (which we cannot fully comprehend) and determinism, and which assumes to disprove that which cannot be proven accept for its own futility in the face of the great mystery of existence.
This oscillation, and often vacillation, between doubt and belief is integral to keeping the system open. We are ever solving ourselves in the world for ourselves. This play leads to the transcendence of former systems into newer, more expansive and complex systems, which in turn will need to be transcended for the same reasons, though the context of the gaps and contradictions will be nascent to the awareness, hopefully. We cannot as yet, and perhaps never will, perceive ourselves outside of this dynamic and expanding and self-referential and porous and boundless self-contained system. Thus the system remains open, is defined as life is defined, but whose awareness is its pinnacle, for now, a clue in and of itself to the nature of existence and its unfolding evolution. What new illusions await in the far future that would crush our infantile minds now, that we couldn’t hope to transcend yet?
As E. O. Wilson suggested, in Chapter 4 of his book Consilience, we not only can perceive 37 orders of magnitude in this universe, we previously and still sensorily handicapped animal, but nature does not normally select for and tends to abhor unnecessary talents. We human animals have far surpassed the evolutionary niche that we initially occupied with intelligence. We have emerged beyond that niche as well as our biology, or at least we currently can.
What’s interesting is that all of the descriptions above in this chapter are not entirely accurate, though true, perhaps more accurate the more these words point beyond language, or even, as we mentioned, the mathematics that Wigner realized was uncannily efficient at describing and manipulating reality. But the closest approximations and cognitive applications and techniques for moving surely through and in reality are by far our conceptualizations of time.  Not all of them of course, for we simulate possibilities in the future and past and present that are not actualized, but for the most part,  time itself, as we understand it, is taken as so real it is reality.
Consciousness is a mirror we hold up to the forest of knowledge and reality. But it is not the wood, nor is it the consciousnesses flitting and hunting and scampering and flying about that wood, nor is it the mathematics we use to describe them.
It is the same mirror we use to look at ourselves, and see endless reflections within that facing. And in the same way we reflect other consciousnesses. And such is the way we regard the universe, and the universe witnesses itself through all of these mirrors, and through our very own mirrors.
Attend to the world. And when you are in the world of thought, participate. Become.
It’s the participation in the attempt to win against a larger awareness in a competition whose demonstrable narrative unfolds into the future and catalyzes and actualizes and fits more truly than any others’, which is a rather futile and consuming endeavor of egos when the light of truth is shown upon it, for reaching for truth is the point, or sometimes simply to show, undress, be yourself to another, but always we should remember it is play, that we mentally strain through and in our listening to all of the potential sounds of our environment and all it resides in and the silence and time that defines its borders. We are in this way perceiving the watching of another of our internal system by mentally perceiving the consciousness of another within that fluid state, even when there is no lover, no friend, no one in the next room, a relative in a different state, but as gods and God, more as the mystery of existence, as being conscious of a world and universe, as that of which we have no words and little comprehension, of that which we are a part, and this thus becomes our awareness of being aware, and by that witnessing you are the now witnessing itself as the dilation of the last witnessing, ever more transcending ourselves and becoming ourselves.
This too is how the universe experiences itself through you.
Participate. Become.

sources.title: Parables of Silence, Part 12, Offspring of Tantalus.txt
sources.content:
Parables of Silence, Chapter 12: The Offspring of Tantalus
Witness
Who the witnesses? Images of you in another's mind. The economy layered above our own, crows and pigeons catching glimpses of your hurried outfit, doves and chickadees drinking the verisimilitude of your movements, the calico cat soaking the entertainment as it looks into the back door, a field mouse scurrying in the night over the grass while you sit and wonder at the stars, flies and moths, secret givers and pollinators upon the ceiling.
All spectators of the drama and they take your events in like a conversation in your head turned obsessive, when you begin to project your thoughts at another and thus lose yourself in the other, as in love, as in illness, and the great weeping that this incurs in the soul is as the great understanding of the other animals, much less so the other apes, who hold parts of us.
It isn't so much so that we are everything we experience, the neighbor's boisterous truck engine, the ash tree swaying, the laughter of our granddaughter, more it is that you are being witnessed by the world more than you know, even as you are the universe conscious of itself, more you are a world, with all its weathered temperaments and watered ideas and rocky constitutions that the great mysteries and little secrets are conscious of.
It is early, and the birds are beginning to sing. What friends will I make today? How will I lose myself and grow?
Most all of us, just based on our physical attributes, are unaware of the medium, the organs of our environment, the contextures that contain us, for which we are conduits as passions and ideas flow and spread within the neural network of this planet.
How easily we spread anger or sorrow or happiness or silliness. How easily are our ideas transmuted and transfered and replicated with or without intention, like catching the flashes of the unconscious, that depth of ocean and its monsters only glimpsed by not looking directly at it, like the worms in our vision.
And it works in other directions too, form only hampered by the intention of the imaginings.
When most of us were children meanings were created or accepted as inherent and were ready-made, natural. As our capabilities grew so too did our responsibility, which is freedom itself, to create our own meaning. Now many seek to revert to the ready-made by side-swiping their minds or through intense play or through reclaiming innocence, and the truth of it is that we are awarded brief moments where the ambience and momentary magic and thirst to persist in being and even to flourish reclaim their right and these gifts are memory makers. But we have centuries of techniques and wisdoms telling us how to get there, from working with and in nature, to music and the arts, to road trips and more; but more often than not, we forget that it is always happening for and through us. We've simply distracted ourselves by our divinity.
In truth, what would we be if given God-like powers, to be infinitely creative and all-knowing? No doubt we'd go mad and destroy ourselves. We are still learning both the responsibility and its freedom and that gods are expressions of the unknown, and in whose place, if we are to survive, will be like the magic that seems inexplicable, when in fact it is simply a technology or knowledge or technique that is not understood. Gods by themselves are definitions in infinite regress. We require these vortices in consciousness to both draw water up from and to keep our fidelity to the unknown and thus keep our conscious systems open. We are a quickening, a shimmering light shone possibly on a far future that looks nothing like us.
There has been a murder, or a kidnapping. Feathers are strewn over the lawn diatomically, in both senses, in intricate alga-like patterns, muddied with a dimming vibration since their ripping, and there is a force in the evidence like two atoms colliding, repulsing. In the last week bully birds have begun to appear, squawking their whinge pap at the dog and at the man. He tries to make deals and promises of revenge. They are unmoved.
The doves' nest in the pine rests precarious and empty. They have all fled except one, who lies upon the wall with heavy, heartbroken squinting eyes. She barely has the will to watch the man watching her, and she closes her eyes upon contact, as if senses will no more save her suffering, as if even the energy that must release itself into action has dulled, even in the immediacy, the impetus of the unconscious predictive processing never making its way up through the flat lake, imaginings not swimming along those insipid, dark from its depth currents, never breaking into natural instincts, subsumed by loss, numb to the fear or unease when a giant bipedal ape comes to check on her.
The imagination, which Sartre said was consciousness realizing its freedom, frenetically attempts to settle inside each scar of the act's aftermath. Relativism aims to slough it off as no ape see, no ape happen, but like the angry relativism behind the pablum of those who would diminish it is in the future afterward, after screeching violence, where something's free voice is gone, where even witnesses and scribes are not allowed, where the right to peacefully assemble has been torn asunder.
Meanwhile the dog digs up worms, ruining the grass, in order to rub that squirming moisture against her jowls, as she wishes to cover herself in life's scents, both ugly and divine, searching for the purest perfumes and connections. The man sits and inhales rolled tobacco and tar, a sometime stem adding to the acrid fullness of the moment. The sprinklers have sucked the blood into the soil that the flies love so much, like the hard keening and lament over knowing so very little. The plants are overgrown. The flowers bloom in rotation. Night approaches. And it's time to go inside, a home no longer available to one taken by natural evil, and a home where one cannot return for its menace and memories, as she sits on block time, past, present and future all happening and all too real, her world's gyromagnetic fields waving and spiralling around her, alone, as the man shuts the door, drawing another marker for spectating the spectre of the undivided accident, joined in the incoherent correspondence, synonym of laughing to the teasing madness. As the man pushes this all back into the waters he crosses the threshold and feels grateful and uncertain and that there is something which he hasn't accomplished and protected, and he assembles all of that loss and focus and is no longer hungry and feels another kind of loss and shame for entering the mundane.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
It is not only that we have a subconscious, for need of a word and its conceptual limits, the dark waters underneath in which we as our egos are in echelon, what we are conscious of barely rising, but also the mind reaches far outward and beyond what we can see and what we envision as our minds normally. Humans attempt to possess that of which they are part while owning their own selfhood. And sometimes humans attempt to possess themselves. How much we truly rely on our animal nature can be seen not only in the day-to-day beating of our hearts and other various bodily functions, but also in our reset back to the original code, nature, elán vital, substance, subjective being. Thank goodness for this corrective, for we too are pieces of the great mystery, and must accept at least some of ourselves as such. What is the mind? Philosophers and scientists still ask this question. There is much more of ourselves than we experience. This is rather frighteningly beautiful.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
If we are all mistranslating what each other are saying into our understandings, then the fact that a person who has learned more in specific areas, who knows the landing strips on flowers, will be more aware than others who do not, and the fact that those others are mistranslating means that they are understanding on some level, and perhaps deeply and meaningfully, suggesting that the goal is still worth it, the goal for the far shore that we will never reach, that we are connected in the collective consciousness out of culturally applicable knowledge, that what we are experiencing as existence experiencing itself is worthwhile and can always expand and grow in meaning and affirmation of life and consciousness.
Happiness is vulnerable. To experience happiness and joy you have to trade in a good portion of your security, for not only appearing weak, but the possibility of being weak, for the sake of joyful gratitude and pleasure. And kindness spreads joy. And so the old adage is tentatively true. And gratitude is an appreciation of life. We value our lives therein. And so we back down from violence, until we have to actually fight for our lives or those lives we couldn’t live without, or the innocent - those lives no one should live without - for life itself. Some also mistake this for weakness. Hopefully that satisfies their hunger, their addiction to willing death magic out of their chests, skin tightening, hyper-aware and hyper-charged death driving their wish for violence. Throughout our history there have been some of us who, either instinctually or what we call pathologically, but always through trauma and fear, take the opposing side, fight for the heat death, and wish more than anything to sleep in the nothingness which is everything without a single contrast.
