We are indeed the most dangerous animal on the planet. And at the same time, without each other and without our tools, fragile and mostly harmless.
Yet all the words to describe emotions are metaphors too, even the word “emotions.” As personal as it gets, we translate our bodies and instincts and transpose our thoughts into what we perceive to be emotions, and these most often are what we sway by, as great ocean waves sway.
Once, scampering under brush from the sight of a dinosaur raptor, was a very small insectivorous mammal that looked something like a shrew. For hundreds of millions of years we were terrorized by flora and fauna of all sizes and tools of death to fit any imagining. And for thousands of years now we have been terrorized and terrified by each other.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Sitting here tonight in my robe in our dark blue walled living room with wood accents and floor, reading in my chair with coffee as Lilly snores loudly on the couch beside me, thinking about how much we identify with what we presume we know and how little we are faithful to our fidelity to the unknown. We hold our identities so often as what we know and our knowing, so that any crack or blow to their foundations will send us and our strictures reeling. But that is never hard to do. Every system of thought is either complete, and therefore inconsistent, containing contradictions, or it is consistent, and therefore incomplete and filled with gaps, which must be filled by faith in expert testimony, and thus the believing component of knowledge. And since every complex system of thought is amenable to crumbling based on the (correct by the way) pointing out of its contradictions and gaps, it would seemingly behoove us to attempt not to marry our sense of self so thoroughly with what we presume to know. A political and moral identity, a religious air of cleanliness and verity and wisdom, a philosophical attachment to an idea, or the clarity of a cultural position and the rest are subject to the idea that whatever system we are working in is liable to entropic deconstruction and persists on uncertain ground, because the system we are residing in cannot prove itself using its own rules. Dogmatism and certainty lead to synaptic and neural pruning. The more certain we are, the shakier the ground. Like the cartographer mapping Van Gogh’s room, they must draw themselves drawing the map drawing themselves drawing the map and so on in infinite regress, as consciousness is ever feeding itself both itself and its awareness of information and experience and ever solving both itself and its environment continually in a transcendent feedback loop, so creative and abstract and reliant of patterns it’s like an art form born from error and the quivering in the equation. The only way a system is verified is from an outside perspective. Thus, it is not only frightening that all of our thought systems are incomplete or inconsistent, not only terrifying that we cannot prove them from within, but more we come to the realization that we cannot prove our own consciousness. It is sufficient but subjectively unprovable. And yet we are herein liberated. For consciousness is boundless, limitless, except for the limits we utilize to create the shapes of the patterns of our thoughts, whose limits are our conceptualizations of time, else we would go mad being so open to all the information available to our senses. Our minds are the music played on the instruments of time. These limits mean that there is always information we are emitting and information we are taking in that we cannot perceive. Still, the trade-off is worth it. Consciousness as a reflection and fractal scale of the whole universe is boundless and expanding exponentially faster into the nothingness that is simultaneously everything as of yet defined by the absence of anything else, and therefore undefined by specificity and contrast, and therefore pure potential, just as was the source or singularity or ultimate variable of this universe - the source as a fractal itself, expanding, as our minds do, into higher scales of an infinite fractal. Our minds only do this not simply because we are as consciousness an anti-entropic force fighting for life in the universe, but by recognizing and being unsatisfied by the contradictions and gaps and the incompleteness of the knowing we now employ. Our ignorance unfolds exponentially wider and faster upon every solution and perception, and we with it, by transcending formerly simpler and less ordered and faulty states of awareness and being. These limits mean that the fractal itself, expanding from every scale and perception, is awareness becoming not only aware of being aware ad infinitum, but becoming aware by that process perhaps in infinite expansion.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
By some estimates, almost a quarter of U.S. citizens claim no affiliation to religion. The secrets of life fall like shadows under performances and that number would be much higher I think. The accepted historical paradigm, however unknown the sources, among many of these "none’s" and their rising ranks among the youth is euhemeristic in the first sense, such that myths are a primitive explanation and exercise for a universe without science and reason and thus outside of the strict logos of reality. It is no wonder then that the reaction, the coupure and rupture of the belief in knowing, is mortified by the hierophanistic binary of the mythos of the strongman king vs the profane, when faith sunders under the need to worship the living, and is matched only by the current enigma of deluded supporters of an anachronistic, illusory sacredness in novel performances and myth-making in which a lack of obedience will procure exile, for every side, and more, ruinations, residing deep within the primitive collective unconscious whose naissance again and again coddles the individual ego and mind with facile rewards and understandings, according to this model. If every one else who disagrees with you is evil, then there is no room for nuance, and societal glue fades until we fit a mold. The failings of scientism as having the only qualified answers to complex questions rears its Gorgon head frozen to strike, reality absurdly irrational in the face of an awakening and freedom. It's a torturous experience. That over half of the population considers profane what a minority in power posits as sacred reflects itself in internal states, fractured insofar as the well of frustration, the synthetic certainty of anger, and strictural disbelief recurs in a bottomless source that both threatens and exhausts those states. That we are in need now more than ever of philosophy and the open scientific regard and the arts to secure foundations and transcend the affront to long fought for truths is apparent. This may seem trite. But these and others are open systems, engender empathy and thus a wider perspective of ourselves, and enable questioning and the ability, by understanding and awareness, of dismantling incomplete thought systems. But beyond even these, as I’ve hammered throughout this text, the choices we’ve yet to make will not only be forever recorded but echo into infinity, and their generation within our minds are not only our ultimate responsibility, but means that if we are to, in the totality of our lives, call our minds home, however transcendent and fluid and fluctuating and unprovable and mysterious, we must be damn sure we are present in them. As them. We must participate in our own becoming. While there is perhaps some truth that myths threaten to put us to sleep and dream the dreams of an horrific past, we must practice and protect our soulful being, for our epistemic hygiene is now extremely lacking, and it's really starting to stink up the place.
Most of we 8 billion human apes are simultaneously the safest and most comfortable we’ve ever been, and the most existentially threatened.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Shadowborn
The closing of a door, a telephone ringing, shame that no vulnerability will redeem, a violence to make the fear go away corrupting, no amount of reason to retrieve the flow where images metamorphic and pale give life upon the watcher, interrupted, no, the past rises like windsong and shade, friends, lovers, mistakes, sublime correspondence and betrayal of countenance and there is only one thing I know, that where I have died, I will die again, I will die, and I will live.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
So many of us want to be smart that the very very few who might not would be a rounding error. Thereof we might safely say that all of us want to be smart. Really really smart. Geniuses even. But your expansion of awareness and the meaning therein has little to do with the cultural facts or the speed with which you recognize patterns on tests. It has nothing to do with status or fame. And here I want to make it extremely clear: that expansion of awareness and understanding and learning and curiosity is entirely up to you. You are the catalyst in every moment to perceive clearer and deeper. And it all starts with accepting just how profoundly ignorant you not only are and will remain, but that you will become ever more ignorant the more you know. In closed systems ignorance is shameful and full of terror. In open systems ignorance births curiosity and learning. The error is what breaks open the closed systems. Your mind at bottom is the mind of the learning animal.
As we lost most of our fur over time, our musk turned to a stink of which when any one of us has not cleaned ourselves for extended periods is almost unbearable. We are compelled to wash away that odor, to cleanse ourselves so often it’s almost ritualistic and definitely routine. Perhaps we spent too much time in water, but probably we lost our - what we uniquely call hair - because of a developing internal temperature regulation that allowed us to run very long distances among other newfound attributes. We traded fur and what we conceive as animal physiology and phylogenetic presentations for the beauty of nascence and the high adaptability and immunology of infancy that came with the cost of not only extra sensitivity, so easily cut by brush, but having to bathe regularly and to give more care to our bodies than any other animal. Our minds work very much in all of these ways as well, not only with hygiene and attention, but that we’ve largely lost the armor of instinct naturally unfolding into the present. Most of the time.
Entropy actualizes and cements what we perceive as events, as time realizes existence, but moving towards degeneration and disorder, and then those events fall back into a determined past. Conversely, life constructs and orders itself and reality by openly taking in and exchanging energy and matter with its environment. Consciousness is not only primarily awareness, life’s awareness, but also at its healthiest pure potential perceiving and creating within and out of and into the pure potential of the unknown, because of which the future is not entirely determined. Consciousness, though needing limits, is not encased by either cause and effect nor the narratives that profess to explain every event, both mentally and physically, to use that false dichotomy to encapsulate differing expressions of the universe, as we need contrast in order to understand at all. Consciousness at its best, so far, is an open system exchanging energy in the form of information and thus meaning within and with an infinitely open system. Time would seal our thoughts in the present toward decay. But that choice is ours alone.
Consciousness without the illness of entropy is the prime force for life in the universe.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
With the birth of dissociation and the default mode network we were able to direct and maximize and sway and influence the microglia pruning of our neural networks. When we close our mental systems, the brain literally begins to damage itself, and the ensuing paranoia and anger and mental cannibalism is almost guaranteed. This catabolism can be widespread  and spark reactionary violence and hurt in the herd and is infectious.
But an open system, to reiterate too much: the human animal can hear frequencies between 20 to 20,000 Hz. These cycles per second of air compression formulate into words and music and everything else audibly assaulting or blessing or boring us or granting us utmost pleasure that we not only understand but feel. This too is analogous to the emergence of consciousness from mere matter.
It is not that you need to passively watch your thoughts in that consciousness to fully accept yourself, but that you must participate in that thinking, you must be and do the thinking and therefore play seriously and take those thoughts to their ultimate depths and logical extensions and limitations and conclusions and the queries hidden within, and then transcend those with other thoughts, perceiving even yourself fully and honestly, just as you already do and are doing so with trepidation and sometime revulsion and sometimes as if caught in a claw trap. Rather, commit, and it will unfold as naturally as any prior states. Even more. Possess yourself.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
When people think-talk to their gods with the music of their minds, say, by pleading to take away a current habit, they do not mean something else that is internally important to them, even though they may simply think, “Take this from me.” Most do not question if their god understood their exact meaning, and if they did, they would fall into a regress and diatribe of explanations.
This is the way we speak with each other using the patterns and universals of language. We assume the connections and shared meanings and understandings, and when there is a misunderstanding, which is inevitable, we spend enormous resources trying to convince the other of our good intentions. Lovers do this much of the time.
Sometimes we do this with ourselves as well. We take time to check in on ourselves, for instance. Most of our time of course is not explaining ourselves to ourselves, for that would be endless madness. But we are ever internally defining ourselves. Solving ourselves, often trying to heal something, to soothe something, to be something more or else, to become. To feed the solution back into the fundamental question, over again and always.
That fundamental question is the wonderment and curiosity first present and presented by your own mind when you were in the womb. And though we are indeed the one’s that are defining ourselves as well, the solvers, so is the our defining the eyes of the universe understanding itself bit by bit.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Yesterday I was called by my mother-in-law to check on a dove that was lying on the ground. She had a broken back and was dragging her legs. What could have done this? She would pass by the next morning I knew. Nothing to do. So we got her some water and put her under a tree where she would be safe and I softly stroked her head and whispered in compassionate tones. And when we came back today she had died. Hopefully she had moments of peace and some little comfort with the west wind gently caressing her under the shade of a mulberry.
She’s still with me in these early morning hours.
We try not to catch ourselves in each other’s reflections. At some point in another’s awareness of us, so focused and concentrated and sinuous and enclosing, when we are backed up to the property line, the ego sacrifice, we will either yield to and submit and present our horrified and harrowing and excruciating naked exposition, or we will begin weaving internal  narratives to clothe and ensconce ourselves and rationalize that spotlight to redirect it, even if but in our own minds. Not only do we not know reality itself at bottom, in totality, essentially, and not only can we not take very much of the reality that we do perceive, but we often can’t even take very much of the reality of other people. As myriad manifestations of the universe shine their lights upon our darkest corners, we will be well secured by the knowledge that there are far more sensory feelers and eyes upon us than we ever realize, including of course the universe through us. Every secret thought, every errant desire, every reaching half-formed exploration. Another almost unbearable responsibility of ours is to witness in turn, to look that light in the eyes and recognize it for what it is. To witness also our own light steady and true, rigorously and honestly.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
And maybe the most heavy-handed metaphor in this entire open ended thought experiment is that just as the source, that pure potential of creativity, could only be defined by that of which it both is and is not, differentiates itself in order to know itself and reflect on itself by creating individuations and limits and forms, and by that constant creation unfolds as infinite expansion back into its own pure potential as space and time and the fundamental forces and all the rest, so is your own consciousness, so is your own mind a perfect reflection of the universe, of existence as we know it.
I hope now that you are aware of your insurmountable freedom and its responsibility you are in and as and may participate more fully through existence. But none of this has been a self help book. May you feel and think on every aspect of life as possible. May you help its expansion and understanding. I hope I have a little.

sources.title: Parables of Silence, Part 13, In the beginning….txt
sources.content:
Chapter 13:  “In the beginning…was formless and void.”
Genesis 1:1-2
“As being is to becoming, so is truth to belief.”
Plato, Timaeus
“When the forest burns along the road Like God's eyes in my headlights And when the dogs are looking for their bones And it's raining icepicks on your steel shore
Well, I'm gonna break, I'm gonna break my I'm gonna break my rusty cage and run.”
Johnny Cash, Rusty Cage
Have you ever made up words to a song spontaneously in part to see how long you could keep the game going while entertaining and surprising yourself? If so, it might be somewhat surprising when you realize that some people not only do this for a living, but some became famous after starting out going to competitions that felt like war while employing this technique. What’s actually surprising though is the thing that breaks up the flow, at one point or another, of patterns for everybody. And that would be becoming aware of ourselves creating and swimming and flowing within duration. Normally the expansion of awareness by being aware of being aware ad infinitum, solving the equation with the last answer, is not only the good but necessary for a wide variety of imperatives.
So, why does observation perforate that parsimony of consciousness?
It is not only due to our agitated and anxious disassociation from the present born from trauma and fear. It is not only because the measuring eventually maps a self-generating landscape beyond those scribes, compasses, calipers and remote sensing. It is also because of the fact that metaphors are representations, however universal their patterns. And it is also due to so much more.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
As time internally slows and a quickening seems to be upon the world and the worlds of other minds it is quite possibly because you are moving faster relative to those worlds.
As we’ve discussed earlier, a cartographer measuring infinitesimals by that measurement would not only take a perpetual amount of time, but the measurement itself would approach infinity as well, and what that map maker would begin to experience is the landscape self-generating over and over endlessly. Therein would they witness infinite variations and individuations forming from and dissolving back into the source, from which is generated new forms. This heavy handed, but perhaps more nuanced than expected metaphor, suggests not only a wider and sharper focus on and in the universe, but a perception of potential experience in an overall boundless system where energy and information never cease to exist, and where consciousness is the fundamental force for creativity and life.
It is perhaps, maybe, that observation in the form of expanding perception and awareness motivates emergence from smaller scales into wider scales and their circumferences of and in the fractal of consciousness, as well as generating glimpses of the possibility that that scale of present emergence is potentially a nested foreshortening and reduction of ever larger scales. We catch the music and scents on the winds moving chimes whose scales have yet to be expanded and grown into. All the while the whole in each scale is both more than any of its constituent parts, and at the same time holds all of those portions and components within it, including the entirety, in this context, of possibilities of choices not taken and actualized. In consciousness’s emergent and expanding fractal the paths not taken also never cease to exist, and are therefore also recorded information, which our unique perspective and agency brought about in the universe. The very act of thinking is a creative force for life.
The French used to say that Americans had therapists because they didn’t have good enough friends. I have some lifelong and close as brothers and sisters friendships. I even have a couple for which the only appropriate words are soul mates, or soul brothers. That being said, I have a bit of a reputation, and sometimes my therapist will give me a brain teaser. Last Wednesday that took the form of the question: If someone didn’t have any of their five senses would they have thoughts? I suggested that it was an interesting question, because at least Hellen Keller had senses she could use to communicate. But there were several problems with the thought experiment. The first being that there are at least 31 senses that we know of, and it’s very possible that the more we perceive of the senses and perceiving, the more there will be.
It is possible, for we cage-eaters, that some of these senses are more strongly linked to the mechanics of consciousness. Proprioception, though ultimately necessary, is not perhaps as strongly correlated or integral to our awareness and its predictive modeling as nociception, for instance.
As Jeremy England notes, what seems random and improbable will repeat given enough time. Like lightening starting a fire. And repetition creates patterns. Our perception, our measuring ever closer to reality without ever getting there, shortens the duration between recurrences by our perceptions of ever longer time spans, though we cannot at present imagine infinity beyond a fraction of a fraction of a fraction of a whim of an idea contained in a few words; but in our measuring and refining definitions and the time it takes to do so, as we perceive more and more intricately as reductively and exactly and clearly in a universe of dynamic change and generation, patterns form in our minds’ eyes, including those eyes, and the world and universe and all the languages we use to describe them, like mathematics, which is the language of patterns themselves and their relationships, patterns and their utility that make up all languages, which are themselves attempting to describe patterns, another kind of living fractal of comprehension and communication.
If we went through a wormhole to a universe where everything was blue, and tried to explain and define the color blue to a blue being, on a blue planet, in a blue universe, we would probably have enormous trouble conveying this concept and universal pattern. This is what it is like everyday and all the time between not only us and the constant mistranslations between us, but between us and the rest of life here as well.
Not only is the internal time spoken of earlier different in each of us, though we approximate closer internal timelines the closer our relationships and time spent together, but the layered clarity therefrom peering into infinitesimals and thus the perception of patterns are sometimes vastly disparate. Sometimes it can feel like talking into a void. Sometimes it feels like being the void.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
So I’m working out a thought, and I think better when I write, which might say something about why so many of us think with and in words and language. Still, it seems to me that almost every life affirming conceptual system we have at bottom is an appreciation and celebration and reverence for existence. In many ways that takes the form of taking  the infinite, in every way possible, and eternal concepts of which we neither comprehend with our infantile minds nor have adequate language for, and attempting to fit them into air compressed in cycles of a billionth of a meter per second, between 20 and 20,000 Hz, and translating it into syllables that we can thereof neatly package into a word or concept, even if that concept is nothing but a metaphor and pointer toward the unknown, for how can you fit the whole of existence and its cause into a single or a few words? And really, if you were to string out the entirety of all of our words together as an explanation of this wondrous profundity they wouldn’t even scratch the surface. Against the infinite our 600,000 or more words are a spark of firelight falling into and becoming the dark. We have so long to go. But some people think that not only is it ridiculous to revere and celebrate and feel a sacred joy at the source of their awareness of existing, of the sheer fact that they are existence aware, in whatever forms they choose to sanctify further through expression the divine nature of reality, and here few words would better suffice, but that it is a kind of slavery to that which is beyond our comprehension and communicative abilities, according invention as it were to honest experience and thought that the cynics and skeptics attempt in turn to shame into sanity by their own logical systems, which cannot prove themselves, like any other, by attempting to disprove thereof something that itself cannot be proven. And of course, as we’ve spoken of so many times, it’s when we think that we have the final answer or truth, that we know, know completely God or the universe or their or its architecture, then the system degrades and decomposes and decays. Cognitively, neurologically, thermodynamically, the mind cannibalizes itself. But at bottom, whether it’s Carl Sagan or Brian Cox or Alan Watts or Meister Eckhart or Jacob Boehme or the Vedanta’s or de Chardin or so many others through these too few thousands of recorded histories, the acceptance of our profound ignorance and the honest awareness of being aware, and aware that we are part and parcel, born out of and remaining it itself individuated, of this existence that we do not understand, the miraculous nature of existence itself compels us to dance and hold our hands high and sing and learn and stare at the data of atomic molecular microscopes and study birds and smile at those birds in the back yard on a sunny day under a soft sun and blue breeze. Perhaps drinking a kombucha, as I am now.

sources.title: Parables of Silence, Part 2, A Progression of Howls.txt
sources.content:
Parables of Silence, Chapter 2: A Progression of Howls
“Intelligence is that faculty of mind, by which order is perceived in a situation previously considered disordered.”
Haneef Fatmi
1
We are easily influenced, by friends and family and politicians and media and art and more. That's why children's and teenagers' friends have more of an influence on them than their family at the time and beyond. That's why every nonconformist in history has ended up looking just like the other nonconformists. And almost everyone falls into some or more, always contradictory social patterns. We are perhaps most defined as credulous creatures, believing animals. Sometimes that takes the form of belief without evidence, and we call that faith. However pejorative that may seem, sometimes faith can lead to compassion and care and forgiveness, especially and apparently for those least deserving. Other times what we call knowledge is made up of beliefs, as in what Plato eluded to knowledge as more than 2,000 years ago in his Meno: a justified true belief. Even what we call true is often a belief in relation to facts about the world. We are very much epistemically dependent on expert testimony about such facts. Most of what we know and rely on both in conversations and how we function day to day is based on this belief and trust in expert testimony. But as we get older, we begin having conversations with what influences us. We watch an action movie and feel for a time that we could defeat a whole army of assassins on our own, given enough rage and skill. We allow ourselves to feel this, but remain aware of our primary but ever evolving ego, necessary as well as illusory, and as the film ends we come upon ourselves again. When politicians speak or news outlets push the panic button we are able to assess their statements as true or false while feeling their emotive quality. For once words were like magic, and stories can sweep us away within their worlds. And with books it gets even better with time. In school one would notice, and often with oneself, that every new school of thought would cast a spell, and for a while it would become part of one's beliefs about truths. But with age comes a conversation with each book, memories of incantations past rising with each word and sentence to dance with the author. But I digress. Like faith there is beauty in allowing oneself to be swept away in a story. But being aware of the sweeping is important. It is doubly so that we garner the tools of critical thinking, the extent to which we can then form true beliefs about facts being the harbinger upon which that thing which is not a thing, that essentially unknowable qualia that is the mind, can form true beliefs. We are believing creatures, and this is perhaps the most celebrated aspect of free will. A freedom proportional to growing older. A freedom proportional to responsibility. A freedom proportional to honest and rigorous inquiry. But only if there is a continual unending process of learning. There is more to learn than any one of us will ever know. How wonderful is that? Learning is the keystone, both for our credulous condition and the progress of our species.
2
“The best thing for being sad,” replied Merlyn, beginning to puff and blow, “is to learn something. That is the only thing that never fails. You may grow old and trembling in your anatomies, you may lie awake at night listening to the disorder in your veins, you may miss your only love, you may see the world about you devastated by evil lunatics, or know your honour trampled in the sewers of baser minds. There is only one thing for it then - to learn. Learn why the world wags and what wags it. That is the only thing which the mind can never exhaust, never alienate, never be tortured by, never fear or distrust, and never dream of regretting. Learning is the thing for you. Look at what a lot of thing there are to learn - pure science, the only purity there is. You can learn astronomy in a lifetime, natural history in three, literature in six. And then, after you have exhausted a milliard lifetimes in biology and medicine and theocriticism and geography and history and economics - why, you can start to make a cartwheel out of the appropriate wood, or spend fifty years learning to begin to learn to beat your adversary at fencing. After that you can start again on mathematics, until it is time to learn to plough.”
T.H. White, The Once and Future King
Taking in information, learning, is the main way to quell the entropy embedded in the very structure of our consciousness, in states of equilibrium (homogeneous states of awareness) and in highly chaotic states, as well as in the foundational state of uncertainty, which pervades all of reality, which I suspect is ultimately unknowable, though we can know ever more of it. Another benefit of learning is that we begin to see things we had never seen before. Not only do new ideas and concepts appear in our consciousness, as when we are reading or even watching a sporting event or political speech or listening to a song, with new information about those things, but a growth of awareness births forth literal new sight, as when a biology student learns about new aspects of flowers, like their stamens and pistils and anthers and ovules, or where the Andromeda galaxy resides in the night sky, or of honey bees and their ultraviolet and green and blue trichromatic vision and the rather common patterns of hexagons and their stability in nature and other interesting bits of knowledge and experience. This widening and focusing of mental and literal sight is of course not relegated to biology, but can be had in physics, sociology, anthropology, geology, mathematics, philosophy, carpentry, welding, plumbing, and all other areas of learning, if we will but devote our time and curiosity. Such is the way with learning, and such are the ways in which we become ever more aware of reality, until faced with the unknown, the mystery of existence that we can never know and never unknow. A natural phenomenon of reality and the universe that is consciousness, which applies to the previous sentence. By taking in information about the universe, information itself being a fundamental attribute of the universe, as well as we who must take in that information in a never ending desire to calm uncertainty and to know, we who are also the universe, the universe contemplates itself in the creative act of self-discovery. As that which is defined by the absence of everything else, it is forced to differentiate itself into endless forms and manifestations. When we learn we are seeing anew and ever more and more, until we are compelled to plow the depths of our own cultures and their histories of ideas and thought patterns as far back as possible, both to see our own selves more clearly, as well as what we know and have known of this existence, and there we begin to see just how little we in fact know.
3
We, as animals, mimic each other, not only as forms of communication, but also as a natural tool of survival. We mimic each other to learn. Reflective gestures are a form of adaption. But the shared story, mimicked through behavior or ideas or speech or all, is not entirely the same story or even feeling within those individuals who are sharing. In such ways the spherical polygonal infinite faces of each individual mirror reflecting the universe for the universe of our consciousnesses, or one of tessellations of infinitely many lunes in each plank of mirror as hosohedrons reflecting reality, are tied by these shared patterns, though they are experienced differently, not entirely homogenous, and this is a consequence of the universe differentiating itself in order to experience itself within and in as many patterns and forms as possible. And those patterns of information are outside of our heads as well, in our language and written words and the use of symbols and art and music, all both received in such ways as to inform ourselves about our own mystery, which is, like the universe, that which we can neither know nor unknow, through experience and the creation of new patterns, as well as projecting our own internal tensions and dreams upon the world which the unconscious reflects on, and we create patterns for the world’s and universe’s consideration and our own. We are information, we as conscious beings create information and we as conscious beings exist as receivers and receptacles and the cognition of and for information, which is in every fabric and texture of the universe, meaning us as well. This information loop of consumption and reception and comprehension and creation and growth is analogous to the reflex-arc of intention, the ever rebirthing of our consciousness, the spark.
4
“I have free will because I could not have chosen otherwise.”
paraphrasing Christopher Hitchens
What is happening to you is largely of your own free will, but, remember, our consciousness experiences backward in time, and so therefore on that flip side, the forward movement of time, everything is determined to happen and already has. It’s both hands, not either/or, in this case. This can be seen when we are feeling something intensely, as with every thought and thinking about and on the feeling, both physical and emotive, and you stop and inspect the feeling and realize this is coming from something else and not your thoughts. It is then that we get a peak at causal cycles and determinations happening in and as the world, their immediacy, and we feel in this emotionally isolated state the forward momentum of time.
Sometimes I’m thinking so much it feels as though I’m getting pushed against my skull, until I get a headache, until I break through and my mind inspects the outside of my body while tying into the strands of patterns in the world. And their charge and agitation and movement vibrates and twists mine own as laundry in the desert wind, and I feel and am the spiral traveling outward and inward from and to the top of my head, and under my right foot, and out of and into my hands, and some strange look transforms my face. I’ve flipped the switch. I am now looking in. And that’s what focus and learning are. Things begin to appear in the world that you hadn’t seen before. Today I was changing wallets and saw that focus and intensity in a picture of my granddaughter when she was seven. Of course, outside of my own internal mind I begin spreading more and more, making stories of the world, which are false and delusional, because thinking itself is a creative act, and outside of my mind the world is already determined, as I then become. This is another clue. Even if we could create reality, not only are we ill-equipped for that responsibility just yet, as we are still too anxious and full of guilt and paranoia and we are too cruel, as we are not predators but label and intimate predatory behavior with evil, and therefore in our attempt to be predatory, we prey animals, we imitate our prejudices and become evil and cruel in our misunderstandings of the natural world, we anxious prey, but we also would, if able, create something with an ending, as we storytellers know but beginnings and ends. But that’s not how reality works, the eternal reflected in us, the open-ended story waiting for us to read it, forever, exponentially more and more in depth, aperta finita.
Our grandmothers tell us that time moves quicker the older we get, and that exponential increase in time’s relevance is precisely due to the patterns of our own minds and those within reality that we give meaning to becoming ever more predictable. Just as we do not normally judge the distance between thoughts and patterns mentally, so too do we gloss over the normal, the routine, the stable and the predictable. And as we gloss over entropy, we gloss over the predictable, both because whatever we build on that predictability we know will be sound, for a time, and because we find great joy and comfort in taking reality for granted.
A seeming cure for this complacency and dereliction and acceptance of absence of presence by so many they are uncountable over the past many hundreds of years, is to live in the now. But it is because we can live within time, narrate and utilize the structure of time as our own, making memories and proposing futures and imagining and experiencing and playing within, throughout, forward and backward, side-like and inward and outward and outside of time, that we have the richness of our conscious lives in the first place. I, of course, have no answers, and as always, this is just a thought experiment; but we might find a hint once again in Kierkegaard when he says that we must become infinitely interested in existence, which it seems may require serious play. Then again the comfortability of the quickening of a predictable life is a blessing in and of itself.
The problem with predicting future patterns is the problem of induction, the fact that we cannot be certain of the future, and no amount of experimenting with reality will cure this incompleteness and uncertainty. This is because we utilize time while thinking backward, know and knowing backward, on the back of time, mentally living and growing and learning and comprehending conversely. This freedom in pockets of time past has gigantic potential. Just think about Heisenberg’s Uncertainty Principle. The more certain you are by measuring either position or momentum, the more uncertain you will be about the other. It is also the case when the wave function collapses with measurement from superposition and all possible states actualize into a particular position. Reality is behind us, in the front, and we as reflections of the universe have enormous potential to make of it what we will. But there is a reason why we can’t seem to get ahold of reality completely.
5
For the gentle to remain gentle, the most appropriate, appreciative, empathetic and thus felt and thus experienced and thus lived, for it necessitates willing light and lightness upon and into and against the onslaught of hardness and stabbing and the burning and the cold and the smeared feeling of insanity and the pain and the sorrow, the willing itself a magic that may, may, save themselves, the most undefined and therefore closer to the source, Atman, God, The Big Bang, quantum soup, The Abyss, The Source, undifferentiated unity, as they could be toward existing, while contrarily the most spread out, the sounds of their minds - those wings spreading and flapping and flying and dropping feathers about, tangled up in patterns and the experiences within, taking seriously their responsibility to create - well, honestly, they had to become clever.
But when one stops caring what the world thinks about them, especially as their mannerisms would not normally make any sense, that panopticon only then pops up several times a day, and that is a lonely feeling at first because one becomes used to the world’s eyes, the cultural eye, the queen’s eye on we the most social of the animals, used to the over-witness and judgment. As it happens less often, a side effect can be confusing situations within public interactions, but this introspection and honest investigation gets one closer to the source, for the anthills of humanity, our own towers of Babylon, are made of sands of belief.
Meaning itself, fundamental to the universe, as we’ve discussed above, gets us closer to reality, closer to being. And as we’ve discussed, the frameworks of information and the meaning residing within also gets us closer to reality. Another way is to run fifty miles, to exert our bodies until we are no longer thinking as such, an analogue to meditation. But in these information loops and living so presently we are close to the forward direction of time, so that both getting closer to the reality of time as well as taking in information thinking backward, and thus understanding the meanings of reality as far as possible, we are ever aware, but refuse to see, that, like the incompleteness and inconsistencies and contradictions of thought systems, so too is reality as we all experience it fundamentally and ultimately unknowable; however, we can ever know more and more of it. We, and our experiences, and the information we take in and create, and the world, and the universe, inhibit completeness and ends and absolute definitions, and we inhabit an existence which is open ended, always becoming even as the universe is becoming, even as time infinitely expands and speeds up. Whenever we attempt absolutism we are bound to fail, for reality despises fixed and static states - as well consciousness, which is the universe reflecting on itself. And so, with this knowledge, fleeting in and of itself, in order not to go mad we must endeavor upon the task of serious play. For we have a great deal of talent in creating and participating with and in this existence, with wills and egos straight from the source, like everything else.
We all assume that we are It foundationally and at bottom. We are the center of the universe, the place where our selves and their consciousnesses spring from and occasionally rest. We are pieces broken and differentiated and individuated from the source, and therefore we believe as much as we perceive that we are the source, It, unified minds, and ego brings from this embrace of being the one. And while illusory, it is also necessary, since it is true for all of us as well as a tool when interacting with other egos and personalities, who are also the one, as otherwise the need to destroy every one who is not you, so that you can truly be the one, would take precedence. Of course egos sometimes do this anyway. And all of this is a reflection of the mirrored internal cosmic struggle, the need to know ourselves and the need to return to ourselves and that undifferentiated consciousness. These are some of many tensions that give rise to the reflex-arc of mental intention, ever circling back, ever creating, intentions defining the forms and limits of our thoughts, limits of which are the patterns of our thoughts, and therefore we experience within the patterns, much as meaning is within information, and those patterns are of the same as the patterns of the universe, how it thinks as it were, and through us as well, reflecting on itself as we reflect on the universe and existence and ourselves and each other.
6
The universe is a mystery to us, deeper than we can capture, because the universe is a mystery to itself, and as such is the reason why it seeks self-knowledge and awareness and self-reflection, which is the reason we ourselves seek to know and create and take in information, which is the thing that quells the entropy of our minds. Our mental instincts are reflections of the universe. This is one side of the coin of binary self-similarities, the other side being a return to an undifferentiated unity, the one consciousness, defined by the absence of everything else and therefore it itself is undefined, as it has nothing to compare itself to, just pure consciousness, nothing and everything. This attempt at the return is time and its entropy. And the farther and faster it travels, that all within it travels, making the traveling possible, giving birth to space and gravity, the longer it goes on toward the freezing heat death of the universe, everything torn apart into nothing, the closer it gets to that reunification. Time is the backward lie.
On the backside of time we display and cast and perform free will like the music that all life that we know of in the universe sings and makes and rejoices in proclaiming its existence in mathematical chords of the mind, reaching from the source to celebrate feeling itself, beyond language, however rudimentary or complex.
And in that free will and its use are decisions in a continual loop, since we can’t choose not to choose, and we choose ourselves and we choose humanity in every choice of ourselves, as Sartre noted. And with that choosing is deciding between ethical choices. Free will makes no sense without regret, that we could have made a different choice, and ethics makes no sense, in fact shouldn’t even exist, without free will. The possibilities of other truths and realities and dimensions and universes is built in this mystery and uncertainty in every direction and home too. Even if we only believed in free will, the belief would be willing-in-action, freely believing, and it would and does matter to our actions in the world whether or not we believe in free will.
God’s, Akasha’s, Indra’s web being born from quantum foam and then the Big Bang, It’s shattering of Itself into infinite forms, was an internal act of creation, with time moving inward into Itself, while we as apertures and extensions and eyes and minds of the universe reflect on the mysteries of the universe and investigate our own mysteries, creating and choosing reality while taking it in as information, the blueprints and fabric of the universe, as information weaves the fabric of the universe together, is the very fabric and its infinite textures, trying always to understand and figure it out, creating solutions like an art form. We are the universe aware of itself, and aware of being aware, ad infinitum, and we inhabit a space where self-discovery abounds in the universe’s introspection, and we get to help in that process. We then, on the forward side of time, are looking out, embodying the mystery of consciousness.
And both directions, both emerging and time based directions, are self-evident.
7
“I think reality is buoyant..  once you figure that out that’s when you’re almost ready for the next level. Perspective is ‘real’ for most.”
Ethan Campbell
“Defined in the general way, apperception is a concept that encompasses every self-giving, thus every intuitive consciousness. Originary apperception is perception, and every modification of apperception in imagination contains an apperception precisely in the shape of this modification.”
Edmund Husserl, Static and Genetic Phenomenological Method
Mimicking is not only learning, but also normalizing behaviors. It is how culture is born. Interestingly, those acts aren’t entirely intentional, and when they are, there is embarrassment at its inauthenticity.
God, Logos, Akasha, Indra’s Web, infinite reflections within each reflection between the infinite disco balls (like Archimedes’s method of exhaustion and the endless lines of ever-expanding polygons to find and make a circle), made up of infinite mirrors of which each individuation is simply a mirror as small as the Planck scale and perfect reflections of the universe infinitely reflected in each and every infinite drops of dew on an infinite web. The waking up illusion would suggest that everything is illusion, but I assure, it is real, all of it, including every errant and sloppy thought. By the laws of the universe, that definitive answer to reality would have to be a lapse of reason and a blundered capture of The Answer, for there is none, and perhaps never will be, though we can ever find out more and more.
Facts about the world and knowledge based on evidence, maybe insurmountable evidence to back them up, when they do not fit the beliefs, usually religious or spiritual, of persons who reject them, will often by those persons believe of them that an evil force, demon or devil, is tricking the world with such knowledge, the talking snake, and inasmuch as the world then changes and becomes more complex and our story richer by that knowledge so will said antithetical beliefs become simpler and simpler.
It is becoming more and more understood by this individual that intelligence is rare on this planet and perhaps the universe as well. 3.9 billion years of uncountable life, including 160 million years in which the dinosaurs reigned, and yet consciousness this aware, however ignorant it may still be and perhaps always will be, is just out of the gate. It also occurs to me that it could only have happened with a prey species who were so constantly barraged by danger, and the danger amongst themselves, from themselves, who had to use and foster intelligence in order to survive, that we have seen such rapid evolution of consciousness, so much so, that most people often cannot believe the stupidity of other people. Examples of this are of course humans and octopuses and dolphins and whales and some birds and so on. Interestingly enough, those of the same family, as in Hominidae, who have had a relatively consistent environment for thousands of years with little danger, relative to us, or at least many moments a day for eating and relaxing, have not themselves seemed to have evolved at a similar rate.
Every empty pocket outside of the sphere of consciousness is itself aware upon closer inspection. The universe is stitched, bound, cast and crafted together by awareness. Still one consciousness reflecting on itself through us. And consciousness and its awareness expands with time. Consciousness emerges, and imaginations are its quickest forms.
Most of the time our stream of consciousness acts as if the water itself were trying to swim, so human-like, water on top of, through, underneath and all around and inside of water, reconciling with the splashing, making currents where there should be none. And there is no water either.
But we know free will exists. We witness it causing and the effects thereof. But we like to pretend that some of the things we experience, like free will and time, aren’t real, whenever it most suits us.
But experiencing has its roots in wonder, as truths being realized, as the very infinitesimal moments of becoming aware of novel information, and living the comprehension, understanding as an act of the self birthing continuously by understanding becoming knowing. Experience is truth. Truth is fundamental to awareness. The consciousness of which it is to be conscious of, that is, a world, and a true experience of that world.
And it is only by accepting everything that we experience that we have the courage for it all. For courage is cousin to acceptance and truth. And we must admit that we are both the sanest and craziest people we have ever known, accepting all possible evidence to support such a preposterous proposition, in order to regain our freedom to play seriously
8
Porphyry wrote that the spirit, like the breath of fire, naturally ascends skyward, but that the corruption of passions brings it down to the damp earth. But the proto-Indo-European root of "human" is "dhghem", which simply means "earth." Though the Earth could have more appropriately been called Water if we were ascribing appellations by quantity, the wise apes' very hearts belong to nature. The mensura by which we meed the benchmark of demesurée reasoning. What Dostoevsky's reminder rejoined together amidst the age of science, that reason without heart is a sin. As Galileo recorded the sway of the lamp of the cathedral of Pisa by his heartbeat, so do we gauge the gibbous nature of normative inner-reserves showcasing themselves throughout every cultural layer and interaction. More than not but not all full of light. The repoussant and the benevolent. The delimited and the capacious. The masks upon the storm. Stones of passions. Living an epiphenomenal existence, much like when we are plugged in to our endless entertainments and consumerisms.
9
I used to suffer so much from being unwilling to voice a defense, a protestation written all over my face in micro-expressions that others apparently wouldn’t acknowledge or were unable to read.
I used to suffer so much from the necessary ego death in intellectual competition. Whether I won or lost, I suffered. Not the base and simple double entendres of, for instance, the inspection of a painting of a ship in a psychologist’s office, nor the possibilities of triple entendres (perhaps even quintuple or sextuple - very rare), but rather more like a schizophrenic losing at Go. Either imaginative, as in, imagine 1,000 individually different footprints in the sand, or definitive, as in, do you know what a gerund is? Or even explicitly requiring a test of skill and acumen, as in, can you solve this problem? But we are not concerned with feeling shame for not knowing, perhaps the reason we started naming, or perhaps not. Maybe that was curiosity mixed with the thrill of knowing through communication. In either case, the point is to feel no shame in that pain of the death of the ego. It’s all learning and an opportunity to learn. We need to find out how it works. We need to mimic the technique. And we idolize no other human. For genius is in the art and ideas and perspectives that change the world, that grow our mental horizons. People are good at different activities and solving different problems and express themselves differently, at different tests of life and respond differently to those tasks. There is no genius animal in and of itself. Clever, smart, adaptable, creative to be sure. But it’s their creations, their ideas that are either genius or dull and obvious. Fortunately, this game of Magister Ludi is very rare, but if one encounters it, acceptance and the ever-learning of life is of utmost importance.
This morning I was looking for something to watch and chose a video on Jung, wherein he espoused that some people, a very few, feel the unconscious mental tensions and shadows in others and groups, and were thus lashed out at, eventually leading to a solitary existence. But we all feel such things. Recognizing them consciously however is another matter.
When we learn we are experiencing, seeing new connections and patterns and depths in ideas and concepts. For instance, if I know the history of Francis Scott Key’s The Star Spangled Banner and the importance of the American flag in the bombardment of Fort McHenry, then when I have my hand over my heart at school or a sports game or church, there is added meaning. This also works literally. If I am an astronomy student and learn about new stars and galaxies, the I will see those stars and constellations and the Andromeda galaxy anew, and my attention to detail will heighten and focus and new sights and information about the world will open up for me. This works with all information available to be learned, with all subjects, on both counts.
But the inherent meaning in the information that informs our very being is always experienced, often without one realizing that they are experiencing, and how that experiencing affects and effects the rest of their experiences, including their conscious-emotive experiences. We want to inspect life, investigate every possible pattern of existence, and thereof become infinitely interested. Our eyes always becoming anew and seeing ever more, both internally and externally.
As we accept that we are pieces of the source, whether that be quantum foam popping in and out of everything and nothing before the Big Bang, or a single unified consciousness defined by the absence of everything else, with nothing to compare itself to and no way then to define itself, and therefore an undifferentiated everything and nothing, who then, in order to understand itself, divides itself infinitely, differentiates itself into infinite expressions of itself and understands itself through the individuations learning about itself through itself taking in information about itself and creating new patterns; as we accept that we are all pieces of one source we begin to recognize our ultimate self in each other and all we come upon, and how all effect all, all of the time.
But the farther we mentally go out from our subjective experience the more false the narrative is, though true for us, for we do not have minds that can see all of those patterns and their interrelationships. And so we assume higher powers are at work. A kind of magical thinking springs thus from true intuition.
What this means, in a sense, is that once again we cannot know everything, and that therefore every part of the universe is in fact the center of the universe, and that subjective realities and their narrative are more accurate the closer to home. Our spheres of consciousness only expand so far, but it does expand, and the free will which is evidenced by every ethical system ever dreamt and thought of and acted upon that cannot exist without that free will, which is directly tied to the responsibility to be participant observers and active creators and learners, which generates that expansion.
And so I no longer grieve neither my lack of communication skills, nor others’ inability to read what’s going on within me.
We have all been on both the receiving and the delivering ends of wit that destroys the ego (and it occurs on both ends), and even sometimes traumatizes. This shame in not knowing is interesting. It seems to be the ego saying, “I am It! I am the Source!” Life feeds on life, competition and suffering and pain and manipulation and harm to others are all acted out as pieces of the source attempting to destroy every other piece in an attempt to be It by Itself. It is maladaptive in the truest sense, such that it is an individually manifested desire of the universe to go back to that unity and destroy reality, in the form of the reality of others, in the process. Strangely, the going back is exactly the point of entropy, and where that decomposition will lead, that is, the freezing heat death of the universe. This tension expresses itself in everything in the universe, including minds.
Consciousness and life itself is the antithesis of this disorder, and uses both time and thus entropy to celebrate life and battle entropy, rebirthing itself continuously, as we give birth to babies to keep our species alive, and sometimes flourishing as a counter active to entropy and time.
In this way time is both the venom and the life, and consciousness more clever than we give it credit for.
Let there be no shame in not knowing or not understanding or not seeing or making mistakes, for we are ever-learning. Si fallor sum. There will always be blind spots. There will ever be more information to gather and comprehend. And when we are undone by our freedom, a freedom innate in being aware, let us take on the responsibility of life and consciousness’s project and goal and aim and reason, and affirm life everywhere and learn from our mistakes and try to know and take in as much information as possible.
If, in these thoughts, you have yourself thought of counter examples and arguments, that is exactly the point.
10
"When a complex system is far from equilibrium, small islands of coherence in a sea of chaos have the capacity to shift the entire system to a higher order." 
Ilya Prigogine
It is perhaps most interesting, that for all the impulsion toward learning, reality, though we possibly can never know it in itself, or the very foundations of reality, is enough by itself, such that every single experience is filled and fulfills being. At the same time the all-encompassing uncertainty that we are alive in and that is alive in all of each of us that compels us to learn ever more, experience ever more, is, in the face and presence of everything which includes our consciousness that is the great mystery, is not a bug, but a feature, wherein we find our freedom through responsibility.
Not only will the mind jump off of, will consciousness move away from equilibrium by paying attention elsewhere, but it will also return to that equilibrium, yet another cycle. However, when there is tension whose viscosity creates so much friction that the equilibrium and an opposing mental state cannot rejoin, for example due to trauma, the natural tendency in the construction of mental patterns is to level up as it were, to resolve the discord by transcending the conflict and contraction to a more profound and insightful experience and state of mind. This in one of the many ways in which Nietzsche’s quote that we are a bridge and not a goal is applicable. We are ever evolving. There are, of course, those whose trauma is so deep and wounds so painful that they travel in the inverse and introspective direction until they can see themselves clearly.
11
“I do not find this existence reflected in the mirror of the mind, I encounter it in life; it is my life, a current flowing invisibly around all my mental mirrors.”
William Barrett, Irrational Man
Unanchored understanding and thought are necessary for authentic mental life and its acceptance of the lack of a fundamental truth of reality, amidst brutal and perfectly true-in-itself existence; no capture of thought by metaphors and narrations here, no language here shaping thought. Only tethered by being and existence, which are the tether and the thought, and therefore the understanding and comprehension is itself always in flux, ever malleable to changes due to its openness and realization that all is an attempt to attain closed systems, to ensnare reality, to apprehend existence enough that consciousness may comprehend it in its totality, to seize its very mystery and why it is that it exists, rather than simply what this existence is definitively, though we doubly want that definition, all of which may be impossible now and in the end. While we may ever know and learn more and more, all the while existence and reality remain unintelligible and out of reach. Our conscious lives are filled to the brim and overflowing with the fact of itself, yet longing and our tiny, tiny minds and sense of self and awareness exist, at least in a few perspectives, in the abyss, the vast empty universe which is our consciousness and which our consciousness asks us to fill, and so incompletenesses and uncertainty remain. We must pour ourselves into time, a bowl that can never be filled, never be captured or arrested, always desiring our nourishment.
12
According to Freud, once, in the beginning stages of our species, words and magic were one and the same. Each syllable casting up images. And as Alan Moore has said, following that, once written words and magic were one and the same. Throughout our short sojourn metaphors and the language that disguises thought, as Wittgenstein noted, have filled us with wonder, cost lives, engendered beauty and torment beyond reckonings, and filtered public dreams through their reflective gestures, what G.H. Mead thought was the origin of language.
Jose Ortega y Gasset
But where is the magic now? For those insensible and credulous creatures who embrace the innate human ability of pattern recognition, vast oceans of conspiracy theories and facile opinions await. Though admitting of infinite distractions for them, meaning lies by the wayside, ever out of proof. For the religious, what William James called the "oceanic feeling", the sublime feeling, can be had, though with many I suspect it requires extra effort in order to sublimate doubt. For others the reconning for a sense of wonder comes about by reflecting on scientific and philosophical knowledge, though this too is as fleeting as all emotions, which we feel with every thought, unless they become part of one's never ending project of becoming, as both Kierkegaard and Jaspers noted. Art that reflects our present state (as Madonna and Child and Giacometti's Thin Men and Picasso's horses reflected theirs), our need for heroes, our embrace of the absurd, our comedic horror at today's irrationality, our longing for the past and our devouring need for the future now, our hope in love, may have the most satiety, as it allows self-transcendence as self-awareness, Maslow's highest level of the hierarchy of needs. And still for others, embracing our nature and being active in nature as the species whose running duration is longest or otherwise, and exploring our instincts and the world also achieve the sense of self outside of themselves. Still others find themselves in serving others, another way to actualize themselves by getting beyond themselves.
In our progress we forget that there are great mysteries yet unsolved, and we founder down into the minutiae of every day and its crises and the hell of its accompanying boredom. But there are still the mysteries of the origin of the universe, the origin of life, and consciousness itself. It's an amazing time to be alive!
Soon technology will remake us, and we will actively engineer our own evolution, with all of the unforeseen side-effects. Yet it is of the utmost importance that we retain our feeling of wonder, that we revisit the great mysteries from time to time and step beyond ourselves. It is not enough to look in the mirror and recite self-affirmations. We must get to the business of living, and that means reconfiguring and searching for that sense of wonder, both found within the search and the leisure to reflect, and become active in the world.
Of course I am saying this mostly to myself. And if by reflection I am to find wonder, then adding information into myself increases meaning and experience and awareness. And so I keep on studying even as information leaves me.
13
William Barrett, who is quoted above, said that when we mean something we are either defining or intending, as in, I mean that this is this, or, I mean to say this. Philosophy, that project of defining reality from the start, just as Socrates attempted always to get at correct definitions, however unknowable (and thus the questions we are still left with), just as such questioning existence itself and honestly looking for answers for being and the universe and all it entails gave birth to disciplines of knowledge, once there was a technique for finding answers within that discipline, almost every intellectual subject was begun with philosophy and still attempts to define its particular areas of reality. Both specialized and general knowledge since the beginning of recorded thought has attempted to define universals and patterns.
We take such a long time to mature, our species, our neotenous relationship with the rest of this planet. These big brains require so much energy, and there is so much to learn. Fortunately there is time to learn, but now in our history there isn’t enough time in one lifetime to learn everything. And even if we could, we would still be enormously ignorant of the fundamental nature of reality.
Because time is the scaffolding and structure of consciousness we tend to learn in the same way as we utilize time, that is, we employ scissions and separate objects in space into discreet units; we divide space into things and segments. We count objects and we learn to use reason and logic and mathematics and history and the rest in steps and discretionary processes. We as reflections of the universe also differentiate infinitely, if it were possible, both outwardly and inwardly.
How much longer for the universe to mature in comparison to we who are so very small?
If the function of an eye is to see, then the mind is to experience and understand and take in information in a never-ending process of learning to know itself and beyond itself as the whole, in its becoming and growth and flourishing, as music is to the earth and possibly the universe, and if anything is meant by soul, it would be this.
14
“And we laugh like soft, mad children smug in the woolly cotton brains of infancy…”
Jim Morrison, Ghost Song
One of the problems of the butterfly effect is that it does not take into account the infinite and immediate intermingling, overlaying, boiling and dynamic collisions and communications between the patterns of reality. Our minds can barely perceive the simplest universals and forms and ideas, which seem to be, but are not by any means, everything all at once. We are at once our perceptions and the objects of our thoughts.
Consciousness most always finds itself satisfied by information and the information of itself, filled and overflowing with itself into the next moment, always enough. Often more than enough.
And just as consciousness thankfully satisfies its own conditions, so is our very evolution and becoming contingent on information, and that information is endlessly available, and to which our consciousnesses automatically and from a source more primal than instincts, applies meaning, makes sense of, narrates and apprehends and comprehends.
The confusion and ignorance and uncertainty that fuels this need to know cycles back once we inevitably find that no story, however well it fits this existence, is closed, but that all systems are either consistent and therefore incomplete, or complete but inconsistent, and as such will always lead to contradictions and, if not their own dissimulations and either concealments or the forcing upon others based of such pretenses of belief, and deconstruction and demolishing, than their transcendence, left to the dustbins and mythologies of the past.
Just as alchemy preceded chemistry, astrology preceded astronomy, humours pathology, miasma germs, and magic preceded mathematics and language, so will our need to predict the future with patterns one day seem superstitious, a newborn’s hopeful misunderstanding, a wistful willing and self-promise to divine prognosticators for power, for a closed system, seeking always to know everything, a wish upon a star.
Such bones of our throwing are as we communicate with our own bodies, their unknowns and needs and our own seat at the heads. Our story unfolds like the cellular divisions of blastocysts and we create solutions like an art form.
We are the seeking animal. We are the learning animal.
15
“‘As follows,’ I said. ‘These decorations in the heaven, since they are embroidered on a visible ceiling, may be believed to be the fairest and most precise of things; but they fall far short of the true ones, those movements in which the really fast and the really slow - in true number and in all true figures - are moved with respect to one another and in their turn move what is contained in them. They, of course, must be grasped by argument and thought, not sight. Or do you suppose otherwise?’
‘Not at all,’ he said.
‘Therefore,’ I said, ‘the decoration in the heaven must be used as patterns for the sake of learning these other things, just as if one were to come upon diagrams exceptionally carefully drawn and worked out by Daedalus or some other craftsman or painter. A man experienced in geometry would, on seeing such things, presumably believe that they are fairest in their execution but that it is ridiculous to consider them seriously as though one were to grasp the truth about equals, doubles, or any other proportion in them.’”
Socrates, The Republic (529 d - e), Bloom translation, 2nd edition
If, as perfect mirrors and their reflections and emergent representations and templates of scale of the universe, we cannot know and perhaps never will know the fundamental nature of reality, it may be because the universe itself can never know itself rudimentarily and essentially and thus reality in itself in its totality by its turning inward and endlessly separating itself, though meaning inheres just as consciousness satisfies its own conditions.
If the thought experiment is to hold, it suggests that we can ever know more and are always becoming, that we must forever expand our horizons outward from internal experience, and it may be that we have to continue beyond our universe someday, for the sake of the universe’s desire for self-knowledge, and this in turn suggests realities beyond the universe as we know it and as it knows itself. Of course like everything here, this cannot be proven. But it’s a simple formula and one that all conscious creatures know: knowledge exists outside of our heads. To be conscious is to be conscious of more than oneself. And consciousness by its individuation from the infinite differentiation of creation is by definition emergent as natural patterns emerging from physical and fractal and exemplary patterns.
If it weren’t for universals and patterns there would be little room for understanding. An example is nature itself and evolution on our planet and across the universe. For instance, the fact that we have psychology at all and explanations for human behaviors and thoughts is evidence of patterns and universals in both ourselves and nature. The evidence abounds such that upon intensive investigation we find that everything that we are and can be aware of, every single aspect of reality that we interpret and apply meaning to as information, is patterns.
We are feeling, we are experiencing within our awareness that faces forward with time, creating most often by imagining and predicting, this conscious life, and it is our responsibility to extend the richness, depth, and insights and sight as much as possible by learning endlessly.
But we become aware of being aware of our emotions when we look back, when we narrate, when we tell ourselves of our conscious lives, and sometimes the emotion is so strong that it is all we seem to be aware of, that sometimes we are so emotionally charged we lash out or break down, so existent we seem not to be aware at all. However, we feel with every thought, and without looking back, but living and thinking forward, the emotive aspect of our reasoning and rationalizations becomes feeling itself, as in, the ambience and atmosphere of our very selves.
The firefly squid has evolved a rather amazing trait involving bioluminescence in order to survive the light searching and invading its waters that exposes it to predators in the ocean’s depths outside of Japan. Interestingly, the squid creates patterns of light across its body such that its very shape is intangible and its silouhette cannot be seen. It uses light to blend with the light in an intelligent act of camouflage, rather than succumb to instincts which would sink its ambitions and fears to the midnight of the ocean’s depths.
In such ways is consciousness performed, while hidden and ephemeral, reflected and highlighted and magnified and created as a shared awareness, while its subsistence seems incorporeal and impalpable and intangible, while its spark of intelligence appears transcendental, almost insubstantial. A necessary and true marriage at scale within the fractal consciousness of the universe.
And as many cephalopods are shapeshifters, so too are the shapes and textures and colors and sounds and manipulations of space and time present in consciousness, and these we call patterns, though between ourselves and ourselves, and ourselves and other creatures, including cephalopods, we see the lights and mental gestures that blend perfectly with every pattern on every scale of self-similar and repeating patterns, here microscopic reflections of the universe, all of which is conscious.
These fractal patterns are amorphous, both diaphanous and performative as gestures of communications, ever fluctuating, and endlessly varied. Patterns themselves can take infinite forms. But when we look at these patterns at different scales we are looking at them sideways. And there time resides, which consciousness utilizes as an engine and scaffolding in order to live and experience itself and the world.
As was said in the beginning, in this experiment, the universe or consciousness is defined by opposites and their interdependence, as well as  fractal self-repeating, self-generating set of impelled instructions, fractal in themselves as they scale from elements to molecules to genetics to cells and so on.
