It seems to me that upon inspection of which species are currently intelligent, such that they order and perceive patterns and communicate those patterns from a former state of disorder, I find them to be either now, or stemming from, prey species. For instance, dinosaurs, who roamed the earth for 160 million years, or any predatory species alive today, are certainly intelligent in the main, certainly aware and have ostensible abilities to choose and plan and be social. (It has been our greatest delusion, one that has led to the destruction of the biome and the annihilation of many countless species throughout our history, that other animals are mechanical, or made for our use and pleasure, or at worst, displeasure.) But this definition of intelligence and thus consciousness is not the same as that of prey species defined above. Most animals are instinctively reactive, fluid in time, reactively fluctuating with the rest of existence, participating in duration without segmenting that duration, as we prey animals have learned to do.
We did this temporal slicing, this scissioning of time into discreet moments, at first by necessarily escaping, disengaging and detaching the mind from the body. We were experiencing such fear, such unbearable anxiety and suffering that the only way to survive was to disconnect, but never completely, from ourselves, and thereof we created the subject/object dichotomy. And in our backing away we defined ourselves in every moment by being aware of being aware ad infinitum. In that awareness was the creation and ordering and perception of that which recurs in space and time, a seemingly organized repetition that we call patterns, which are also reliable to our perception, and so they are predictable, and because they are predictable we infer them as information, the content of which inheres with meaning.
As I’ve noted previously, the shapes and sounds and textures and colors of our thoughts are the limits and perimeters of the thought patterns themselves, and these we create, not so much by cutting time into frames, but by sculpting geometrically potent patterns out of time itself. If we did not have limits and boundaries to thoughts, they would be pure noise, infinite potential and therefore unintelligible. The limits we uncover and create by the process of intention, an intention that is our attention on time, analogous to a mantissa and significant, such that that floating decimal can narrow the focus into many splits within seconds, or remembering lifetimes, or contemplating the universe as much as our minuscule minds are able - a gargantuan range exists in the sculpting of time for our awareness. For instance, if I pluck a string or vibrate lips within an infinite and endless vacuum, the sound is fast lost. But put that string on a guitar, or as lips vibrating through a trombone, and in a room of your choosing, then we have frequency and tension and mood and the music that inflames temperament, as Morrison announced. We thus have patterns.
Our great sojourning and domination of the planet is not because of an innate need for conquest, or a will to power. All of our surviving is due to strategy, and we as former and still, a prey species, have been problem solving for at least 300,000 years, probably closer to 8 or more million years, like it’s an art form. We still carry those anxieties and dread and panic and suffering with us. And the maladaptive traits associated with so many, doing harm to others and selfishness and cruelty, are as an illness, a byproduct of being prey, that is, a pretending to be predators, and in that act taking what is natural from it out of the equation, turning it into evil, as we call it, a consequent from that early separation creating the subject/object duality, that is, the ego. The necessary illusion.
But this is countered internally by regret, or conscience, which informs us, in our looking backward, of choices we could have made differently. This affirms our subjective free will, and thus all of our ethical systems, by this belief that we could have chosen differently. In the past everything is determined. But we take that knowledge gained from regret, for most, and live forward with a vigorous and redoubtable amount of potential in our choices, because responsibility is integral to our freedom.
Of course, there is an antimony to be addressed. It could be, as this human animal, this believing and curious and seeking and learning and uncertain animal, that the metaphors by which we communicate and think that we spread over an as of yet unknowable reality, is just the narrative we experience and create and perceive, all the while moving and being just like any animal. Perhaps just beyond the sphere of our everyday awareness the refractions that perceive us, we who are then unaware, see an animal in motion, instinctively reflexive and reactive, yet whose gestures are uniquely that of the human animal. We can show the subjective existence of free will, but we always have to live with the phantom limb of determinism. Perhaps one day we will become totally aware of this contradiction, and ascend passed this to a wider circumference of awareness and ordering of more complex and honest information, marked by its fidelity to existence. We may never reach the ultimate truths, since our consciousness is an open system, exchanging information and energy between itself and its environment, expanding at the expense of its environment, and leaving behind entropic waste, as is the rest of life.
Consciousness is nothing if not both the epitome of life and has the imperative in its own survival to be life affirming. Closed systems of belief and politics and ethics and philosophy and science and mathematics will lead to mental entropy, because in those closed systems, by being closed off, there is no exchange, and no new information to stabilize the system, and the energy will slowly or rapidly dissipate. The uncertainty, the awareness of ignorance, the doubt, the error because of which anything exists at all, is the drive and engine for growth and expansion, the drive to learn, the drive for life. Learning in one form or another, is the only cure for mental decomposition and disorder. And yet, that part of consciousness that is not directed toward learning and creating both the self and out into the world, will feed on itself, closing the system, taking too much of itself down the sieve toward entropic waste.
I have noted previously that if a system is complete, it will necessarily be inconsistent; and if it is consistent, it will by necessity be incomplete. When a system is complete in this human world of our making, the other way we find ourselves, as we have mentioned, by instinctively searching out and finding these contradictions, which are inseparable from complete systems, and by the simple awareness of them, we transcend them into a higher order of perceiving and ordering more complex information, and that cycle continues.
When a system is consistent, there will be gaps or truths that can neither be proven nor disproved, nor can certain truths or falsehoods be proven. Within this social belief system based on trust that is internalized and from which we build and find ourselves, there will always be gaps, or truths and falsehoods that cannot be proven. There we must have faith in experts, or experts defined as such, qualified and accepted mostly communally, but by our own longing. And so we both become ourselves by exceeding ourselves through the awareness that we are participating in our own compulsory becoming through believing this human world with all of its simplicity, and we find once again our freedom there, in the believing, and there a wherewithal of a higher standard for what to believe. We call this process critical thinking.
And we face requisite and obligatory contradictions in every mental system of belief, and there we find our freedom too, in the responsibility to seek out those conflicting and incompatible truths, and by becoming aware of them we surpass and eclipse that once closed system and enter another whose circumference is larger and wider and expansive, and this process, on both counts, will happen as long consciousness exists. They are structures in the evolution of consciousness.
c)
No one really talks about the worlds they see in each other’s eyes anymore, the You've seen things, the great sadness and wisdom. Maybe all the shine is gone. All the depth unsunk. Everyone's distracted. No more old souls. Everybody’s new. The game of positing scenarios for future reference in order not to offend would in a world so polarized, so ineluctably trigger happy lead to madness. No, no. It's because we've forgotten how to look, to really look into someone's eyes. I once taught an enforcer for the Hell's Angels how to read. That man looked you in the eyes. I once had a fellow homeless woman grant me three wishes, which I capriciously used and which came true. She knew how to square the weight of the soul with the accompanying intelligence in the eyes. Folks got their feelers out so far they're having conversations well outside of themselves and ignoring the immediacy of presence and facts. Take a glance. Shake the thinking backwards off and jump in. Witness.
Imagine a lightbulb that can get infinitely brighter. In the process of its increasing illumination the walls of the room that contains it recedes infinitely into the dark, the black which the lightbulb attempts to penetrate always and forever out of reach. And shadows play in that penetration, that dance of photons and certain immeasurability. And there is no room either.
As a prey species we have evolved and learned to manipulate our environment. We have learned to hunt and to build and to keep and pass on and down knowledge. So are our choices, coming from prey species and still retaining many of their emotions and instincts, not simply mental flinches or flight turned inwards. And in another way they are exactly that. Our minds by this conjecture are as a collective disassociation. But in that realm we make our mark and take our ground.
Anticipatory strategies can backfire stupendously and astoundingly on our pattern recognizing consciousnesses. This is our utterly unique neurosis and genius as a species. We have a tendency toward repetitions, which are foundational to patterns, and we not only perceive and order but create patterns. We call these habits, which most animals have; but ours are self-propagating instructions that we are always attempting to resolve, we open systems. Our conscious lives are dynamically unstable and of and for which we are continually stabilizing. And though uncertainty is our natural state, and once again highly entropic, ironically, we fight this entropy by fighting certainty and accepting the uncertain foundations of reality as we understand them.
We pay deference to our evolution by wearing wolves’ skins. And the most simultaneously amorphous and concrete things we know are our thoughts.
A cell dies off if it does not exchange information and nutrients with its environment through its cell wall. So do patterns of thought , and thus the life of the mind, as living, as life, needs the same. This is an example of emergence and gestalt, just as patterns of thought are more than their consistories and constituent components, and therefore exist as much more than the time consciousness relies on for its very existence.
And so too do we need the world, and more, each other. We are alienated and lost and unmoored from one another because we are certain, about ourselves and the world and each other. But by embracing our uncertainty, our natural state, our vulnerability, our ignorance, we are better able to approach, to witness.
d)
“The deeper natures never forget themselves and never become anything else than what they were.”
Kierkegaard, Fear and Trembling
Much of what we assume is the free exercise of our will and choices is habitual. In the ways we have previously written about how consciousness glosses over predictability, so too do we burnish and polish our illusory egos through these repetitious codes or self-propagated instructions that makes of free will the veneer of plastic upon the living and organic born from the affirmation of life through every choice, and thus closing ever and always these systems of semblance to actual freedom. Or, this tendency toward closed systems and defining ourselves easily belies the potential that ripples through existence with each and every choice, and how we define ourselves informs those almost effortless recurring decisions and behaviors. Behaviors because we are always and forever choosing ourselves. And like choices, we can’t help but to think, because that would be a thought, and the thought that attends that one and so on, and like our thoughts, and like our choosing, we have an interminable responsibility to make choices and to think towards goals which are life affirming for all, even if that initiates patterns of choices which become uncomplicated and undemanding, as if we’re watching ourselves choose ourselves and our actions, which is commensurate with eternal recurrence. There are lifetimes in life. And we can’t help but choose their replicating patterns from the start. We must. Here. Now. That’s how much responsibility we have in our freedom. That’s how much potential there is in each choice. Infinities may spring there.
3
In the binary and dichotomous nature of reality, we posit endless becoming and choices as the progenitor of time itself within the sculpting and  scissioning of time to make of time the scaffolding of our thoughts. And this temporal and geometric slicing is endless because we never get to become anything. Such is the dynamism of the life of consciousness. We are seemingly never quite aware that time precedes and would exist without and gives birth to consciousness naturally, and eventuates into consciousness, even as we accept that we are the subjective creators of temporal experience, while at the same time viewing time as an external movement and duration, and consciousness eternal and at its core without beginning or end. This is another reflection of consciousness as a perfect mirror of the universe. We are nothing if not contradictions, ceaselessly and boundlessly recognizing ourselves as such, as we again and again ascend or widen or dive into new paradigms, with a more ordered set of patterns and more information and thus meaning.
4
a)
Too does the west wind pool around diaphanous gestures, lambent reckonings. So too does fear gimp the foreshadowed gaze, the bristle of the skin's breath not matching the lung's, each impression choked in the skipping. So too the compulsory agitation, the prayers and weakness succumbing after the option of creaseless timidity, then oblivion and the tightening thread to the heart and mind from trembling knees self-fulfilling. Too the rocking, the sweet bereft, the grip of lucent hands on the chin without the support of muscled strings and the lost image from which they no longer sink. The phantom limb of fate and the mendacity of the will's lack and soucouyant. As with this night's totem vestiges, so too do bruised souls hide behind fools' hats, roaming my lands, making sure those stakes were secured.
b)
Thought is coterminous with choice, accept what you choose in thinking, which is also perhaps what both thoughts and choices are, namely, each other, becomes whole worlds, ever fluid, protean and diaphanous and ephemeral, but with color and sound and texture and definition. The insubstantial part of thought is its substance, whose defining is far more ancient than language. And these structures and ordering of content, and the thinking of it, are awareness being aware of being aware.
But like an old dog who buries his bones in the couch, nose scraping the cushion over and over - drawing blood there - so too can we be and get ensnared by a thought, panicking and entrenched and seemingly chained to and reflected infinitely in the mirrors of our own selves, awareness stuck in loops, as all of our strategies are for a finished state.
But that frozen awareness, by being aware, is now holding that thought. We suffer so much because we know it is an illusion, but are imprisoned there only by our lack of sight. We hide and tremble to look at this thought which we are so separate from but which is in fact simply us. And with catching sight of it, quick fertive glances and then bolder, we then transcend it, as we secretly knew we would, because consciousness is also coterminous with time.
And although we imagine we create our subjective time, given the chance, time will show its precedence, and sweep us up and on. And we are impelled to think another, to choose new worlds and universes, self-contained but infinite, an open system by the fact that it exchanges energy and information with its environment. And internally that environment is the awareness of that awareness, ad infinitum. And therefore internally the mind is self-generating and fluid and open.
All animals choose. The beetle chooses direction when you block its path. The elephant chooses to lead. The ape chooses its thymus tap. The starfish sidles through coral. Our instincts have and continue to save us. And they will again, sweeping us up and along.
And one of those instincts is choice itself, who we have stood on with one leg while playing an instrument to create and respond to the language of the universe. Or rather, the world in which our thoughts exist nestles so comfortably in our bodies.
Being stuck in a loop of thought is anxiety, is panicking. It is the neurosis of a former prey species, which we have evolved from precisely because we are ever and again and more and always aware, a disassociation out of time, in both senses. We are an ever openly unfolding consciousness aware of itself.
The fact that that consciousness in this form can do this while also interacting with the world, changing that world, making a home and world on this planet, leaving the planet, while also taking care of its body’s needs, while having endlessly dramatic and complicated and beautiful and hurtful and agonizing and profound relationships with hundreds of people, in a world of 8 billion, is nothing if not astonishing.
c)
One of the many collective delusions of our species is not only that we are not animals, and not only that other animals are not conscious, but also that our very being is separate from our awareness.
This can be stated in a simple assumptive proposition, as in: I am good naturally, it is a part of who I am, and not because of my expanding awareness.
That proclamation of course misses the entire point. If learning and growing and expanding in this world and universe are presumptive of living itself, even the simple and constant act of learning and adapting to our current environments, then being in that world and being conscious of that world are the same. My being is my awareness, in which the natural phenomenon of my consciousness is the most intimate thing I know, and therein is my awesome ability to choose both myself and the world, is my knowledge of what is right and wrong, is my compassion and empathy that comes with understanding, and is my way, the way I move in the world and universe.
And we are seekers and students, all of us, on the way.

sources.title: Parables of Silence, Part 7, Like Infants We Were Born Old.txt
sources.content:
Parables Of Silence, Part 7: Like Infants We Were Born Old
“True.  All too true.  I have never been at home in life.  All my decay has taken place upon a child.”
Henderson the Rain King, by Saul Bellow
I will sweep away the dust that settles on your dreams Je balayerai la poussière qui se pose sur tes songes
Barbara Pravi, Antoine
The Holy Water
No one lives outside the walls of this sacred place, existence.
The holy water my soul’s brow needs is unity. Love opened my eye and I was cleansed by the purity of each form.
Saint Francis of Assisi
“Life is one member of a class of phenomena which are open or continuous reaction systems able to decrease their internal entropy at the expense of free energy taken in from the environment and subsequently rejected in degraded form.”
JD Bernal, The Physical Basis of Life
Some have said that the Now is the only real thing, because no one has been either to the past or the future. This is so obvious as to be redundant. But it’s also indicative of a viewpoint that would close off almost every aspect of our lives, from the time it takes to walk a mile, or clocking out of work, or watching or listening or reading any narrative, as well as all that went in to the printing and publishing and delivering and buying of that book.
Perhaps one of the most acute lessons for what our minds do with time, which is real time and real scaffolding and patterns and real consciousness of those, as real as it gets, is that we need subjective testimony to tell doctors our pain level, or to tell a psychiatrist what we’re experiencing, and so many more examples. Information that is necessary for treatment and communication and empathy is largely based on subjective truths, which wouldn’t be possible without our scissioning of time, and the truth of our experiences therein.
To have the experience we need the constraint. But the danger is in believing the limit is the limit of what we can know, that the metaphors are reality, that the temporal sculpting and thus the form is only that which encompasses, and encompasses all there is, that an ever-evolving and expanding awareness and mirroring of the complex and rich understanding by transcending patterns has no voice over reason as we know it.
Our minds were born to manage fear and predict danger. Anxiety was the catalyst for the emergent and open system that is consciousness, and still persists in between the frame rates of temporal slicing. Anxiety exists on and in the dark and unknown. We can direct this energy where we choose. The potential is manifold.
Between animals the mimicry of physical expressions signals and induces empathy. It’s the same with the human animal. We call this reflective gestures, a mirroring of states of being, as in neural mirroring, like catching a yawn but across the board, which is a micro-loop and fractal of the languages of theology and science and common parlance and the rest, as these are smaller scaled worlds and tools of consciousness, which itself is such with the laws and forces of the universe and the universe itself.
The point here, made uncertainly, is not to get stuck in the patterns of a specific scale, nor closed systems, but to see as many of the relationships as possible, on every scale possible, while also embracing and accepting our ancient biology with our embryonic minds; to actively participate in our own never-ending evolving, forms and patterns and information that will doubtless drive us temporarily insane, over and again, as all of our temporal existence is a continual stress test. Here a regard with the attitude of serious play will come in handy well into the future.
When measuring the Cliffs of Dover, such that you have a ruler measuring 8 miles from the International Space Station, the approximation of measurement is more than the ruler due to the nature of the coast’s wavy and jagged shapes, and upon closer inspection and measurement, like being transported to Dover’s beach, you would have to stretch out the whole yardstick, all of its inlets and rocks, not only because of the dynamism of the changing water and thus shore, but because the ruler is a tool used simply and effectively and the reality must be manipulated to fit it, as in the stretching of the shore into a straight line so that its length can be measured, and it would be much much longer than the rule. What’s more, the ruler does and does not measure itself. And if you measured with a molecular ruler a single molecule of those cliffs, it would approach infinity. So are patterns at scale and within their fractals reaching for infinity the closer and more receptive one regards, gives attention to and perceives and is the seeking animal, the learning animal.
Although like most things existing by definition, and therefore by contrast, which are the limits of those patterns which we scission and sculpt out of time, patterns in general are recursive and therefore you will eventually come back to yourself, that is, present awareness to yourself, have awareness of yourself, consciousness mirroring itself. We sculpt out of time because we disassociated from ourselves. As happens when someone is severely traumatized by fear, they get stuck, they freeze, like a cornered rabbit, or deer in headlights, and here was the separation of the mind from the body, though never completely, in a soft bellied, clawless, unarmored, fangless little primate that was apparently delicious and much of the time hunted. As a snack. We only became the hunters, the explorers, the conquistadores, because we began replaying our past and the total value of our lives in a moment of regret for choices that could have been made. We replaced instincts with simulations, immediacy with planning, and though born of trauma, these birth pains gave rise to rebels in life’s battle against entropy and death by consciousness ordering the disorder and learning, and life will rebirth itself hopefully again as again for eternity.
We became aware of our free will this way, because we could have chosen differently, and turned our attention to the present, and perhaps more importantly, the future, where we were able to predict and plan and actuate our own movements ahead of time. This feedback loop of attention is the reflex-arc of intention, which has real world consequences for and in and out of this world. And the entirety of this awareness is an awareness of patterns throughout. It’s how we move and function and learn about ourselves and the world and universe, a negentropic, anti-entropic force for life and consciousness itself, keeping the dynamic system from closing like a sealed off room, leading to death, but rather open and exchanging energy and the information that is that energy’s organization and intelligibility, possibly forever.
It is a terrible weight, this responsibility within our freedom, this swimming upstream. It comes with the territory, and we can only see the map. But our ignorance is the ignition that starts and utilizes the tools of our minds. As this book mirrors what you perceive in and from it, until that system opens to new patterns and relationships, so does awareness mirror itself to itself and thus to the universe. We are just at the beginning, still babies and toddlers, messy, emotional, unaware, hilarious. But nothing and no being on this planet learns as rapidly and as thoroughly, while remaining open. If the learning and curiosity stop, the universe will devolve back to the unity defined by the absence of everything else, and therefore nothing and everything simultaneously, but without knowledge, without definition, without creativity and without even a mystery unto itself.
And there is endless mystery yet to be sought, to be discovered and for which there may be solutions and patterns beyond our current comprehension, over and over and again for eternity.
These subjective normalities, like language and thoughts themselves, are created in timelines, like everything else. These subjective normalities, and the fact that we notice this, that we organize time externally and internally, is no evidence whatsoever of Now being the only reality. Everything in our awareness, including processes themselves, belies this absurd reductive and obvious statement. We yearn to return to the flow, the ever present awareness in time. We desire a return to being without control, a deterministic flow state in flux while having an immediately responsive will that displays and utilizes all of our talents instantly available and unfolding. But we are an animal who disassociated by intentionally slicing time into thought patterns, moving so quickly and abstractly mentally that we are even able to predict to a point, all because of the awareness of being aware.
Interestingly, when we wish to be in the flow, where everything seems to happen as they should, we are in one sense surrendering ourselves to our fate, just as we delusionally view “lower” animals controlled by cause and effect. As an acceptance of determinism within ourselves, the terrible weight of the responsibility of our own free will fades into the background as we perform and often wonderfully. And so it is that not only is regret proof (of something that can neither be proven nor disproved) of subjective freedom, but the anxiety of such responsibilities and the errors it engenders that are requisite to learn and grow exemplifies, at the least, our free will. In the space and connections between our minds and bodies we have room not only to create, but act freely in accordance with the predictions and ordering and acceptance of perceived reality. We have the freedom to be aware, which itself engenders freedom.
What we have hitherto created, all of our systems and arts and music and writing and sports and media and knowledge and beliefs, may in the future seem as quaint as a child’s drawings. We of course love those drawings, but there is so much more to express and generate and construct. As we develop so will we create forms yet to be imagined and thought and yearned for.
When we do get a clearer peak at reality and existence and being, when our thoughts take a more luminous path and regard, which is both instinctually veracious and more consciously aware, we immediately surrender ourselves, forgetting almost instantaneously, so that we do not have to accept and experience again and remember the profundity of that snapshot of the great mystery, which is beyond both our capabilities and is too much to feel, the next stage on which we have glimpsed and seen how small and infantile we truly are, and we return to the creation of our ever becoming, shaping the water in between the stimulus and the response.
Our very being as awareness, birthed from its separation from its body, out of time, anxiety, the reason for its creation, followed us, and we are ever in a state of wanting to reconnect. And when we do draw ourselves closer to the source and time, whose boundaries and movement and energies and possibilities for creations are the very building blocks of our consciousness, we feel safe and sharp and easy, almost achieving symbiosis with our own scaffolding. But upon a closer and intimate inspection, we are horrified, overwhelmed, maddened by the intensity and immediacy and wonder of it all, that which is beyond our language and comprehension. We have made a pocket between being in time and out of time, out of time. And so we fall away back into ourselves, back to the ego, back to the creation of ourselves, we, who are the center and of utmost importance. In total reconnection with the body lies trauma and memories of trauma and overwhelming information, and because one would experience everything, feel all the radio waves and molecules of air and vibrations emanating from the earth and electricity in buildings and photons dancing passed shadows, all of it, one would then experience nothing, as it all would be undefined, and so my attention on my coffee cup excludes most available data.
As bones of our throwing are working on divination all of the time, working on old woes, working on predicting danger and making ourselves better, so do we perceive our mental control of our own bodies, with divine command and sometimes a hyper-focused localization, and at other times a hard-to-accept fumbling, as well as total vulnerability in that desired control. This too is a feedback loop.​
The orrery of our chimerical consciousness, born from supernovas and stars and a planet, puts its eyes in and gets snagged up on a solar flare, nothing but ashes afterwards. But there is breath in the water. Breath in breath. Taliesin knew this. Hafiz knew this. And so many more. It takes in breath your eyes, and your secrets give flight, and there the love and the hunt.
In that pocket built of and in and out of time, consciousness, like the universe itself, like time itself, is self-contained but without boundaries, except for those limits it frames to understand the world and universe, and, of course, except for its own body, which it too transcends most of the time, even as it continually transcends itself, born from and out of this planet and universe and entrenched therein, only encumbered by itself.
Ensconced, ingrained, transient, impermanent, nondurable, furtive, flitting, this exemplification and typification of life, this embodied consciousness is not even a prototype, but a blastocyst, and thus a pigpen or polybius or route or more ciphers even unto itself.
Consciousness was born in hominins by sculpting time, and thus there is always a certain pneuma-nous agitation that ever restarts the fluctuations away from the present, ordering what was once disordered through patterns and contrasts and limits, and there is then meaning and subjective free will and information itself, which, if Landauer is correct, when it is destroyed, leads to irreversible destruction and entropy, like a book that is burned, dissipating wasted heat dissolving into entropic equilibrium in the form of ashes. Conversely, following the same logic, and Brillouin, information would then be negative entropy or anti-entropic, because it exchanges information with its environment, which we call learning, which is life affirming. But unlike Brillouin, we do not want to see a reduction in uncertainty, because we want the system to remain open infinitely, which requires doubt and uncertainty and humility and no certain answers. No certain answers would touch reality and existence at this point anyway. The infinite universe is big enough for that: information is amenable to change.
I’m thinking on this cool dark morning in the desert that as we naturally and wishfully and sometimes unconsciously mimic, so are we iterations within our species. We often call this culture and subcultures. Often this shows up physically, with what we wear and how we wear ourselves, our hair, what colors we unfurl, our gait and swagger, what holes and drawings we put in and on our skin, and our expressions. But the most consequential iterations and thus reliable and predictive repetitions and thus patterns are our ideas and thoughts and perspectives and the beliefs of we human animals. Herein we find the myths, the absolutes that are both the cornerstones and keystones, the foundations and the critical limits of culture.
If our culture is our world, our world tells us often and consistently that we are things that need fixing. It commodifies our impetus to ever become, but attempts to limit the choices that engender that becoming. Doctor, homeless, lawyer, teacher, engineer, fast food worker, person of faith, addict, mentally ill. It tells us that we must remain present, even though we can’t help but be, save for our consciousnesses which have the amazing creative ability to move about in time and utilizes time to create itself. It tells us so many more myths.
It’s no wonder that we are so neurotic, each in our special ways. But even our neuroses are like children playing with building blocks.
And yet we are so interesting even in our self-loathing and assumed special status, both creators using the magic of language like it’s as easy as child’s play, and a pockmark on the agitated face of things.
And yet in all of our contradictions and immaturities and worries and imperfections, we are so very wonderful, the human animal. So simple, so complex. So insecure. So contradictory.
What a gift is consciousness.
Our anxiety and depression and hyper-attention and overloaded senses and obsessive behavior and schizophrenia and paranoia and emotional deregulation and alarm bell consciousnesses are considered things we normally need to numb, either through entertainment or altering our minds and bodies or taking drugs that pathologists deem appropriate and necessary. But this breath inside of breath, this agitation that moves us, even the confusion and angst that remind us we are alive, this biology that makes us breathe when we attempt to hold our breath, is not only the force of life, but these errors in the system are the very reason for human consciousness itself. That is, 3.8 billion years of evolution, and more, 13.72 billion years of the naturally unfolding instructions of the laws of nature. And sometime far, far into the future we will have new and more interesting and intense and playful neuroses, interesting misappropriations of the disassociations of our minds, whose insanity, which everyone has in their way, but which culture and society tames, but never fully, for the taming isn’t instantiation of empathy, but performative rule following, should be aggressively and humbly accepted as natural to our evolution, in order to direct these qualities that arise from constantly scanning and trying to predict and find patterns, toward learning and creativity and solving problems, both externally and internally, like an art.
I have seen great changes in people based on the metaphors of language constructing their internal worlds, for we are both participants and observers in our own and ever becoming.
I need those who've known me and those I love to remember the things I've forgotten, and this works both ways. As such I am whole and inextricably a part of the world. In fact everything, the stars tonight, what I'm watching on television, even the car passing by are who I am. Everything that I've ever experienced makes me up; everything I have ever been conscious of is my consciousness. In these ways the mind is outside of us, in us, thoroughly strewn throughout.
Eyes evolved to see light and shapes within. It is an organ responding to and adapting and regarding its environment for its own benefit and understanding. Organs evolved to match preexisting reality in a biological process and rhythm preceding the “I.” So too are brains and thus consciousness with regard to meaning and learning and patterns and predicting and narration and exchanging information with their environment. So consciousness understands and imagines. So language defines by its contrasts, and meaning inheres within, and because it is defined by those limits and recursions, like all patterns, it cannot quite reach reality. “Beautiful” is not the sunrise, and neither is “sunrise.” None of the words printed herein are touching the truth of things, but are an experiment of play unfurling perhaps a blueprint, a suggestion of the beginning of an idea, a spiral surrounding a vortex that perhaps vacuums our ignorance by pulling that engine toward curiosity and a way of thinking, an exercise in serious play.
I’m suggesting that the evolutionary scars that we attempt to numb are our superpowers and the reasons themselves for consciousness. That the limits and boundaries are necessary for the music, but that they can also become the cage. That the transcendence of those patterns and closed systems, or almost closed systems, is the evolutionary purpose of consciousness. The ever-expanding consciousness perceiving truer but never completely reality and existence. We do not perceive time as we believe, as a river external and outside of us, and nor are we on the edge of reality peaking in. We are a center of reality, and therefore conscious of its core and a core of consciousness. Interestingly, because of the limits we create in order to understand at all, we separate existence into differentiated units and relationships, that is, information, which, ironically, because of those limits, means that reality is forever beyond our grasp.
We are the tension on the elastic connection between the fundamental forces of existence, and we are the negentropic force that may save both ourselves and even the universe itself.
Information is the reason and content and context of matter and space and time, insofar as information needs for its very existence mediums by which it may be received and more, understood and created and more, become aware of itself. Patterns, potentially infinite in shapes and relationships and scope, are essential, such that the mediums recur in space and time. The more and deeper and playfully perceptive and closer we inspect the particulars of patterns, the more we see. The show enjoys witnessing itself; the sculpture and sculptor are the same.
Here we simultaneously stripped away stories we tell ourselves, while using those same words to explore the possible mechanics and utilities of certain ideas that at our current and nascent stage are lacking veridical proof and verification, one way or the other. We are only able to do this because the universe is not symmetrical, there’s an error in the system, there’s a wobble in the equation. As such we must always keep the system open. We, the uncertain animal at our best.
In the same way we order the world and universe, and feel, a song is defined by its relationships between instruments in time, but the feeling of it is what it is, and we feel that subjectively, so it is that mathematics, which is the relationships of patterns, and the patterns of relationships, does not tell us what “it” is, its essence, its existence total and defined, and so does our ordering and narration truly of existence and life not touch existence, but we feel it, we know ourselves within all of the innumerable relationships of existence, we know our essence throughout our experiencing, we know ourselves subjectively and true, we know itself, which, only judged by its warlike defense and the horror of its loss, is one of the most valuable modes and tools and techniques we have; it is one of our most prized possessions.
All of our language, mathematics, physics, common parlance, theology and the rest, are fractal mirrors at smaller scales of consciousness and of the universe, insofar as they are self-contained but boundless, without borders or walls or limits, ever-evolving, within which we create limits out of time in order to know anything at all, as definition necessitates contrast. And time is simply another perspective on those self-similar fluctuating and recurring patterns, which we name fractals. How lively and vigorous this universe and existence.
Through the recursive and reflexive arc, or feedback loop of intention and attention, which is consciousness, which itself allows for self-reflection and a subjective self side-stepping time, an act created through and out of time, and thus awareness, patterns too are not simply  recursive in and of themselves, but are defined by their relationships, not only what their contents are but what they are not, like looking up words in the dictionary and the words that define those words, and so on and so on, until the original word appears again. We can see infinity in this recursive nature of the relationships of patterns, and look into them endlessly. And feel them.
These patterns are mirrors to our own consciousnesses, and we see the universe and the Source/God/Universe/Abyss/Atman/singularity/metavariable/meta-metaphor etc. in ourselves this way, and in other animals and life, so that the universe can announce through us, “Here I am.”
Every point in this universe is the center. And it is all expanding exponentially fast away from all of those centers. What we call the Big Bang was not an explosion, and that too is not the right metaphor, in space, but of space and time themselves. I think about that sometimes. And each of the centers are reflections of the universal consciousness, that, in order to understand itself, since there was nothing outside of itself, no mirror to reflect on itself, created the universe as we know it by dividing infinitely, like dropping itself as a mirror that then shattered into infinite pieces, all reflecting and looking at each other, like the funhouse mirrors at a carnival and their endless refractions, all of which is the universe experiencing itself, distorted differently in each of us, just as each jagged edge of our shards are shaped differently. We are the nerve-endings of that which is beyond language and comprehension. The nerves are not only our feelings and sensations, but our conscious experiences. Language, which when heard from afar seems so violent, moves us more than music itself, and is its own neuroma, sheathed and open and self-contained, a growing raw and exposed extracellular matrix, self-referential and recursively frayed neurons that build whole worlds from its simulated axons or metaphors. Creation is self-contained but open, and to use a word I’ve beaten to death here, infinite, which means that since all of those mirrors are open systems, they can always exchange information and energy with their environment, forever, while excreting entropic waste. As long as we are learning. Or so I hope. As the function of the eye is to see, so are the functions of the mind to learn and create.
“Consciousness is the last and latest development of the organic and consequently also the most unfinished and weakest part of it. From consciousness there proceed countless errors which cause an animal, a man, to perish earlier than necessary…If the preservative combination of such instincts were not incomparably stronger, if it did not in general act as regulator, mankind must have perished through its perverse judgments and waking phantasies, its superficiality and credulity, in short through its consciousness…”
Nietzsche, The Gay Science, 11
But we are just out of the gate. We are just opening our eyes, the sleep in which was only recently licked clean by our mothers, and we are barely cognizant of this existence. We cry after this birth of 300,000 years, so little time in this universe, and we are just beginning to coo songs and stories not yet written. The last to evolve in us, consciousness is still clumsy and slow and altricial and visually impaired, and if left to its own devices, without accompanying instincts, would have meant the end of us long ago. That’s how young our minds are. In such a state we must honestly assume that existence is fundamentally unknowable to us at present, and this should drive an insatiable and joyful curiosity and interest and uncertainty and experimentation. But we don’t even do that. We muck about in self-referential and inadequate metaphors that we blanket reality with, like throwing an ace of spades into infinity and declaring that we’ve won our infantile games, these “coils of grammar” of metaphysics, that we are the end-all of creation, that all of change over time, all of evolution, lead to this baby babbling. We are just waking up. Who knows what wonders await? This ignorance is not shameful, but the engine that drives our never-ending quest for discovery. But first we have to accept just how profoundly unaware we truly are. We are so early to this party that we have barely begun to experience the great illusions that will need to be shattered.

sources.title: Parables of Silence, Part 8, Where Are The Feathers Gathered….txt
sources.content:
Parable of Silence, Chapter 8: 
Where Are The Feathers Gathered…

….where the songs and sweetgrass, where the caress of laughter and where poems of prescience?

“We reached the old wolf in time to watch a fierce green fire dying in her eyes.”

Aldo Leopold, Thinking Like a Mountain

"Penetrating so many secrets, we cease to believe in the unknowable. But there it sits nevertheless, calmly licking its chops."

H. L. Mencken, Minority Report


Eyes turned to listen with trembling
as we use other senses to describe senses,
the color of music, the feel of taste,
the images of scents, each door knocked
by the hand of riotous distractions.
Oh give us this day, our daily
rubbing of the head, skin stretched
in spirals, eyes the color of skin,
somnambulant in the present,
red raven in the chest skipping beats
with each terrible thymus tap
of sunken thought, overcome.
Oh give us each day, our daily
fear-feasting, our daily
gut flayed and red-assed from the reproach
of gracelessness.
In the time it takes to run
others' thoughts bankrupt the moment's
ambience and that which is taken for granted,
the formation of memories by its feeling,
in its stead a cacophony and its
thumping, tamping down the soul
as rain digs up the earth
and smells tell of danger and panic.
What is this prayer for if not
to divine the water's edge?
In the interim what sweet seconds
of silence sooth and betray the onslought.



You will notice that throughout the day, participating and swimming in society, no doubt with a sense of frustration, many people make bad choices that sponsor immediate irrational reactions, because they have not, or could not, foresee the patterns that would unfold from such decisions.

But that’s not what is frustrating. For that we have their presumption of innocence. Playing the victim with a belief that injustices are being hurled upon them. What Goethe suggested, that intentional ignorance is one of our greatest sins.

Like all social interactions, this creates ripples of anger and frustration and anxious reactive lashing out among the moving herd. Of course, positive interactions work the same way, as does the feeling conjoined with perceptions.

Analogous to these observations are questions such as, Why did only a few species evolve more complex and reflective emergent awarenesses, especially our form of it? What is the difference between us and elk or squirrels?

Interestingly, although most of the weapons of predators seem naturally designed for sole purposes, for whether a claw is tearing open a belly or marking a tree, it is still singular evolved for slicing, while a shark’s teeth or tiger’s fangs are for ripping flesh, the defensive weapons and armor of prey species often serve multiple functions, as in antlers or scents or quills and more. We, who lacked all of these defenses, were left to run and climb and hide. We were too slow and filled with protein. “Mostly harmless.”

Intelligence proved itself a niche that we filled not only in order to survive, born as a trauma response, but our ability to see patterns also served multiple functions, such that it increased our adaptions because we adapted so quickly, and thus increased genetic mutations, so that we are now essentially mostly hairless baby apes, bipedal and makers of songs and guns and medicine.

We look much like newly infant chimps, with the neotenal features of our species, because not only are we an infant species, but because, ironically, we take so long to mature, precisely because we need so much protection. This feedback loop of our internal emotional inflammations and bursts of ten second fight or flight responses are exactly what evolved into this need and accompanying abilities, why our will power fades away, why our emotions fade, and why we assume our own innocence even when we make the mistakes which lead to intense negative wave blasts.

Unlike other prey species, some of whom can stand and run within minutes after their births, our function is to learn and create, and that requires time, in every sense. And that requires protection, and all of the above requires community. This can be witnessed in other mammals with large brain to body ratios, like elephants and orcas and sperm whales, where at least the females stay largely with their groups for their entire lives, eventually serving the role of grandmother and that role’s function of passing on culture and wisdoms. We, as the most social animal on the planet, have an ever widening community to support us, but still need some semblance of an immediate family, though that is not always necessary. A question that might be asked is, what happens when community and culture and myth and art and wisdom and knowledge are replaced with closed systems and empowerment of and embrace of those forces which are antithetical to life and consciousness?

Another interesting idea is that the functional multiplicity of what can be a weapon, or armor, or strategic or predictive ability or perspicacity, this morphing quality of consciousness which adapts to the information and energy available to it, is that alongside its creative solutions to problems that will always generate anew, it creates art. Once safety is temporarily guaranteed the surplus of mental energy finds other outlets. As soon as photography was invented, and for that matter as soon as writing was invented, or anything else, we played with them, we used them to make art, that reflection of existence that attempts to capture itself beyond words and comprehension, as in selfies and modeling and movies and The Epic of Gilgamesh.

So it was with language. So it is with artificial intelligence. So it will continue to be, because inherent in existence experiencing itself is the need to communicate that experience beyond the bounds that allow for experience in the first place.

If in every moment is a choice or choices, then in every moment multiplicities of possibilities are generated and may potentially unfurl like a fern leaf ever unfolding out of every possible space and direction into new spawning potential developments and all of the countless possible choices therein. Time opens and reproduces infinitely, instead of simply moving forward in the same confines and direction. Time is self-generating and harvesting itself from itself in a miraculous formulation of something analogous to a piezoelectric autogenesis and exponential expansion into the infinite. Time is always infinite steps ahead, though we may grasp one or two or several before their fleeting. This is what the limits of thought patterns look like, and this their shapes. Like the fact that our emotions are also patterns, which we can read instead of simply feel, so are the very contents of our thoughts, though very much uniquely ours, however mostly influenced as one of the most social animals, also localized fragments of the information that is time. As this medium of information, that is, time, is simply a certain reflection or surface property and entropic force of matter, which is another reflection of information, more solid and visible to be sure, if viewed in a certain way from a certain scale. Time is coterminous with consciousness, and time is coterminous with matter. All forces between which whose frictions are essential for the universe’s existence, which may be the process of understanding, refracted in the greatest wisdom that the elderly possess.

All of this is most evident in our thoughts leaving us, tied or not tied to the unfurling of spontaneous novel thoughts old as time, our thoughts’ diaphanous and protean and amorphic substances themselves. Time moves ever quicker beyond us, while we sculpt it into the limits and shapes of our thought patterns, and that can be in any direction, never able to predict completely or without error, and therefore still vulnerable and raw and alive.

As emotions can be read, and here we now know the reason we feel with every thought, so can awareness be aware of its own thoughts, and so on, and so too are emotions the translations of the information of the body and information affecting the body, and thus a limitation of the trillions of its component pieces and their relationships, as the body is itself an interpretation of more fundamental components, and so too is the universe aware of itself through us not only by we literally being it, and it being us, but more by reading and comprehending and understanding all the rest of that of which we are unaware, even if what we are unaware of are both our own inner workings and higher or outer workings. On this scale we can only read and understand so little, for now. How the universe does this, just like how we do this, is construed with matter and thus space, which share the same boundaries and extensions as time and the other forces, and this we call reality. Everything is consciousness. Everything is meaningful because meaning inheres and is the context of information and the feedback loop of its reception. An error fueled expansion toward understanding that we call reality.

In our space we’ve carved out of time, by time and of time, that is, our minds, which are thus also localized extensions fitting the larger pattern of existence becoming aware of itself, creatively making of what it pays attention to while it can what it intends. 

In this pattern, your and my patterns, by imagining and simulating outcomes, by predicting as accurately as possible, the mind not only is able to see the possible patterns of the past, and is therefore able to regret, and therefore prove its own free will to itself, but can also see possible outcomes of the future. It is some of these possible outcomes that we all mimic in others or ideas or myths. There is a great pleasure in imagining yourself elsewhere and when and who, in pretending to fit the mold of another pattern or patterns, a piece of the infinite whole who and which is necessarily a part of your pattern within the whole, the reason it can be mimicked, because there is a space you call you. This becoming or embracing or acquiring or molding over, out of, in and through and from and into, is the medium that information travels through and by. It becomes. The universe expands and becomes. We are participant observers in our own becoming.

The surest embodiment of the connection of utilizing time to look back toward the past and the disassociation due to fear and terror, is a human animal looking over their shoulder as they speak, and we say if they are looking behind them to the right, it is the truth. In regarding the past the mind is also trying to spot errors in the choices made, and it’s the same regard turned toward the future, as needing to predict is also simulating potential dangers.

Imagine a wife with whom you are always in trouble for eating the cheese. After many years of your absolutely and apparently uncontrollable addiction to this high caloric and fatty and so very tasty dairy product, you no longer feel so guilty about your evolutionary drives, but use the same eye roll with upraised hands, not in supplication to the goddess of shame, but in exasperation, yet riding the wave until the moment, which never seems to, passes. In these limited expressions in our physical repertoire that we all have and use, as many different experiences are embodied and performed as can be imagined and more. 

And that’s how real it gets. The limits define you, but the information of which and in which and of which you witness and create is infinite, and infinitely interested in its own existence.

———————

One of the great tricks of individuated consciousness is that when the collective showcases itself in the narration of coincidences, it is not the message that matters as much as the being with which one projects, as the presentation of one's story, as innocence is recorded. It matters not what narrative you project onto the often bizarre nature of reality, due in no large part to our microscopic understanding, but that you can make a choice therefrom at all is miraculous, and both are themselves information from which the universe experiences itself.

Trusting your instincts, and that your instincts aren’t only thoughts, which is something our thoughts instinctually are repelled from moving toward, until it becomes them, something they fear, by paying attention to not only what we are impelled to and thus our instincts, we begin to move more naturally, surely, immediately and immediately understanding and quicker and slower when necessary, a falling back into oneself, the one at the edge and at the center of abstraction and patterns, the whole and the tiniest point.

The mind radiates playing with abstract patterns and the more abstract the wider the circumference. Or, perhaps this is just the case, that the ripples of reactions to foresight and the subjects of awareness encompass the great phenomena of existence, that they are not aware of themselves until the moment consciousness begins to exist, that its awareness is able to hold the idea of time primarily and with a larger circumference than its existence, and that the phenomena of itself is of an instantaneously informative and absolutely qualitative property.

Instinct in this regard is transparent to time, perfectly encapsulated in it, and the awareness of our instincts are never of time outside of itself. In this way instincts are primal and at one with localized time, trusting the outcome of the next being of ourselves by trusting that part of ourselves that continues despite our being conscious of it. And if all else were to fail, if some monstrous hand were to sweep away all existing things around my being, I would trust that my instinct would inform me to make myself the subject of consideration, and apply myself to that nothingness which was now in existence.

The consciousness of a self is nothing without correlations to existents residing in time and space, because it cannot be fully what it is conscious of, yet it is everything that is the awareness of time and space and extends itself fully as such, fulfilling itself spacio-temporally and thus but another example at an attempt at perfect reflection. This is one of many ways we know there is a world outside of our minds. And as thoughts extend, to give instinct the requirement to transcend thought and yet surrender instinct to thought would thereof be irrational.

To give instinct a place in experience and yet insist on its transcendence of knowable experience is to have irrationality as a part of the construct of rationality and the cognition by which we act. 

What’s more, an honest attempt to further the understanding of instinct is irrationally swept away by the need to conceptualize it within the bounds of experience, and therefore we cannot fully understand instincts at this juncture, and therein it is given free play of the imagination. 

When we wish to fall back to instincts, we not only lose sight of the infinite expansion of time, we become perfectly encapsulated by and in time. Not only our thoughts would surrender themselves to become instincts, but the nature of our disassociation is such that, just as we are forever out of the garden and nature by that very disassociation, so would our singular embrace of only instincts be an excuse for being, an attempt to strengthen the non-possibility of being something else - nothing.

The mistake is made when we believe that our instincts are reality, as we understand it. Or, will eventually become reality. Instincts are as prone to error as every living system.

It might be that the more that we know, as the sphere of awareness widens, so will we more and more speak and write and communicate about similar things, not only analogous ideas to each other, not simply resemblances, but matching and so correspondingly reflecting that they of a piece are identical and equivalent. I do not mean here thoughts and their creators and experiencers, but much of the context of those thoughts, their ideas, the story of everything and the story of us in it, assembling ever closer to unique and original versions of the cumulative collective.

——————

Just as all life on this planet is related, that the other animals are our cousins, that the Pine and Ash trees in our backyard are even more distant cousins, which we can prove genetically, all the way back to the last universal common ancestor, so is all of that which exists in the universe, and therefore all life elsewhere and outside of this planet, also related.

Just as recognizing that we are in a box is perhaps one of the most potent tools we have to transcend the box, or cage, or limit of a thought or thought systems, and so ourselves, so is by being aware of a keener and more expansive perception or regard or intention we not only can become that intelligent exploration and play and experimentation, but implicit in such cleverness and recognition of new scales of patterns is the possibility that they too can be transcended. These are some of the ways in which the human mind evolves.

In this never-ending process of becoming, I'm never sure if I'm continually becoming anew or myself from the beginning. Living both at once is the spiral-path.

—————-

One of the reasons that conversations are circular is that we do not normally remember the fuller story of memories or concepts without each other. Sometimes it will be years before we see certain people again and revisit those memories, which much later elicit the same but spontaneous laughter or sadness or contemplation. In such ways do we move in the world, rarely noticing but a fraction of what exists, and what exists within ourselves.

——————

From the source, for some examples, the Big Bang, or abiogenesis, myriad and potentially infinite forms possible and actualized interact as some of time's dimensions, namely, possibilities, endless and with potential profundity and experience and meaning. In such a universe, observation itself is interactive, and relational, as with everything else. 

Strangely enough, it seems that we, and most life forms that we can tell, sieves of information, itself possible forms and aspects and inherent internal structures and meaning within the awareness of matter, cannot comprehend or be conscious of all that information, both from within as without. And perhaps that is because we might lose our minds. More likely, if we were able to be entirely conscious of ourselves and the world and universe, we would be reminded, would be confronted with the implacable evidence of our own nature, our insanity evident, and we'd laugh at our egos and the thirst for power and the lust for distractions and fleeting pleasures. Better to cloud the mind, we mistakenly think, as if trying to sketch an ever moving and fluctuating cloud, to siphon the bits we need to function and little more. But within the nothing between us and ourselves, the intention and the reaching and the serious play and whatever forms of the imagination by which we conceive, construct and thread the tapestry are as much the universe we find ourselves in as the cosmic web is as the neuronal structure of the brain, an analogy as well as mathematical fractal, in that it may be endless, may be part of something infinitely larger, may hold all that we can imagine and more.

To be honest, being admitted to this world is a struggle and a gift. Above all it is very, very interesting. And I suppose it's not so bad we're barely out of the gate, our species, the ancient beast in the naked ape transformed into what they call the mind, round and round the cage. In this way too we are very early.

What is required for play? Is it the safety, the pretending or suspension of belief, while also lightly holding rules for the game? Is it the unfiltered and loose time and objects being played with? Is it the motivation and low stress or is it simply the secure place where fun is readily available to be created and experienced? Is it more?

Conclusions, solutions, theories, stories themselves are not in themselves closed systems leading to decay. For that we need dogmatic and rigid and immutable assumptions of truths or the truth. One of the ways this is evident is that authors’ works are seemingly and endlessly interpreted, and this is especially the case for religious texts. For example, how many denominations of Protestantism are there currently? Interestingly, even as a given text is endlessly interpreted, the belief in that interpretation’s truth is implacable, and therefore closed. This is not only with regard to religion, but to scientism and political identity and possibly every complex system of thought. A fascinating side effect of this is that it is assumed that at the next stage of evolution of the mind for adherents is the ability to choose whether or not we need regret at all, that is, a corruption of living itself, because this apathy is part and parcel of the decomposition intrinsic to the entropy of such closed systems.

We are not the only animals that play. In fact, it is safe to say that most animals play in one form or another. However, whether or not Orcas are playing when wearing seals as hats is not altogether clear. And many animals strategize and plan and have rudimentary understandings of time, and therefore potential stories of themselves they tell themselves, in whatever language or understandings those may be. We are not the only conscious being on this planet, nor are we the most special. Our evolved consciousness is not unique to us and not necessarily unique at all. Certainly we live in and outside of time more than most. Certainly we are often and maybe the only truly cruel animal, because we simulate the suffering of another and still inflict pain anyway. Certainly what we create and who we are, are filled with beauty. But we are not above any other, in the sense of specialness or divinity, even though life must feed on life, and most certainly not in being the learning animal, nor the animals that believe, though we express and expand these qualities farther as far as we know. We are as sacred as life itself.

————-

With the disassociation of consciousness, we normally assume that awareness is separate from being. This is blatantly false. For us awareness is being itself, being expanded, being aware expanding, expanding awareness.

sources.title: Parables of Silence, Part 9, Cullusions and Collisions.txt
sources.content:
Parables of Silence, Chapter 9:
Cullusions and Collisions

I stared at a tree waving at me underneath 
sparse full white clouds in azure skies for 30 
minutes today.

Today's chilly weather contains getting closer 
to the Pacific, salty and crisp, layered 
beforehand with purple and orange shrubs 
upon sand dunes.

The sun can still burn you in this paradise,
the least when you're not paying attention,
friendship with the buzz of mystery
and romance, sleeping beside the highway
shaving in the lifeguard showers,
protecting a future drowning,
rinsing off waves so fierce they
hurry their emotions deep 
and feet that carry into the motorised.

Myriad atomic crystals sunlit and in no 
particular order swallows skin so hot
one side's got to give, and in the sinking 
surrender the land cools like 
the nature of lust, sleepy, urgent,
whiffs of oud and moonlight upon the beach,
a wooden place, eager, inviting, 
and you forget that you too smell
of the earth, beyond and behind
the sun protectors, that you too
are giving even as you're taking in,
that you too are a reckoning,
a choice, a becoming.

Now in those waves are your tears 
at such ferocity, letting your body 
be the ride, saliva and nose drooling
intertwined that have just as much
import to your cells and menagerie
of bacteria as the bathtub full 
of water we've investigated 
compared to the oceans
of the universe left unattended,
even by the listeners and their arrays,
even by the light seducers and even
when they're the emitters,
crashing, coalescing, the rising crescendo.

As the wave leaves your body
on the sand on its back, 
you are drawn back in.

But that's how it always is:
once you encounter a bigger reality,
once you converge and emerge
without some of yourself while filled
with more than you thought you
could handle, you become both
smaller and as big as the universe,

and all of this tied together, drawn back
the tapestry woven from the sea
so far away and so close down deep, while 
counting our winged brethren, the wet dark
soil, the chimes as happy soliloquy
marking each inhalation of this pipe,

one makes their way as they find the way,
as I come upon myself like a jaguar 
camouflaged as everything else, and on the 
entering skips a few beats
and flies,
always in contact, always a smile
when I come home to drown.


There have been too many books for me to count over at least the last couple thousand years about how we should try and be present, and achieve living in the now. Perhaps actors come closer than most when they are performing. In the creative process we feel most, and are most aware, possibly. Isn't it funny how our awareness spans time and struggles to be in the moment? Labor and Ponos, always.

If dark matter is a cosmological constant, and the universe is flat, as both seem to all appearances (or effects), than in probably much, much more than a googol the universe will begin to experience heat death. The universe currently is expanding, exponentially, faster and faster, and we call this inflation. Eventually, it is surmised, it will expand such that even the strong nuclear force will disband and the components of atoms will fly apart, there will be no temperature differences and therefore no thermodynamic free energy or "work" done, and there will thus be the Big Chill. Dark and cold. However, it is possible that new universes may be created within, as well as some other potentialities. 

But how are we to figure it all out? How are we to save ourselves and survive until then, but even survive until our star dies and expands, engulfing our planet, and then survive that? How are we going to survive ourselves? 

We have all these instincts from millions and millions of years, but infant minds that make stories for them that don't often fit. We have gained power and wealth and material things and control, but the planet is correcting course and we're as unhappy as we were at most times in our history. We are the most social animals in the world and this makes us susceptible to strange and simplistic and false stories, and our beliefs help to give added life to them, and our emotions are susceptible to manipulation, too easily, and we get addicted to rage and the anger that fear engenders. We are easily swept up in movements which do not benefit our species or ourselves, and internecine conflicts rule the day, all the while relishing our individuality and it's place in society, largely chosen from the lifestyle options of each generation. 

But it is when we are aware of being aware, and creative, and learning, that we truly own ourselves as a part of the gestalt. And perhaps that's what it's all about, the universe, matter, becoming ever more aware of itself as itself, and as itself in itself, producing and creating and adding to the story. According to the conservation of information, the minus-first law, the no-hiding theorem and more, information never ceases to exist, but simply alters in its becoming. And perhaps such is the way of our ever becoming and learning as an open, anti-entropic system. Perhaps each hidden thought, as well as the rest, records the universe becoming aware of itself through us, which is itself.

Beyond these things I would suggest that those things which advance us and add to our ability to direct our evolution in the best ways possible, make us the happiest and most fulfilled, and adds the most meaning, which is the context of information, and warrant further investigations and research and study, should be endeavored upon. As a start, another piece of advice that has shown up for thousands of years, and that even I relate to our granddaughter, is that kindness, love, compassion and empathy should be embraced whenever possible. These are open systems. The simulation of placing oneself as another is another kind of breath within breath. I would also suggest science and mathematics and physics and poetry and philosophy and the arts, but I may be biased.

Sartre famously said hell is other people. I was driving back from Culver's tonight, jamming out to tunes, thinking about thinking, when I spotted a man in front of his house on our street. I waved, and the man gave me a dirty, intense look. My mood was ruined, and I could feel my mind beginning to create scenarios where I would come out on top. Ah, and so once again, another ego death. So many, every day. They actually hurt. Isn't it funny how we're a part of this collective, how we get the most meaning from our relationships, and yet there's the overwhelming presence of other people again? What power they have as we integrate them into our awareness and thus consciousness, as well do with the whole of our awareness of our environment. This is more evidence of the free will of others.

And thus back into the fold, forced into the present. A present we are always agitated away from like an irritant, and into the wider and deeper perspectives of time and meaning and narration. The present saves us over and again. It’s embrace is essential to our very survival. Yet we are forever drawn and seduced and create ourselves away from it, through and by patterns.

———————

The same medium by which the forever infantile ape makes thought patterns, that is, time, is used in the same way by which consciousness is born and expresses itself across the board, and in the other animals as well. Navigating, strategizing and predicting, time moving throughout matter.

We assume other animals, though there are probably millions of different reasons for it, possess the same rudimentary awareness, based off of their behavior. We assume the scaffolding is the same. But our own attention and intention scissioning time into the shapes of our thoughts are analogous to temporal topological iso- and homeomorphism, a self-contained but open system that can stretch and shrink and bend and expand such that even circles can become squares in this dynamic and geometrically adaptable and continuously fluctuating consciousness when required, a mapping function and its inversions continually generating itself in equivalency between mind and body, body and mind, through the ever expanding and reversible infinite possibilities of time, which we utilize as surfaces which can be shaped therefrom into the patterns and their shapes and colors and sounds and textures of thoughts, and wherein choices are actualized.

The other animals are not seemingly exiled, not only periodically but for the most part, from duration proper, and as such need no proof of their own volition and free will, as evidenced by the sickness, after they’ve lived free for a good time, that accompanies their confinement and cage when caught and trapped. And yet they are sentient and aware and conscious. They strategize and predict and remember. They have culture and tools and social bonds. But our own terror, and terror of not knowing, has uniquely lead us to search existence itself for answers and truths. We must find only our subjective proof of free will in possibilities and thus regret.

Normally, when we see someone with what we might call a crazy expression, we tend to think that the expression itself is what is abnormal. And yet, conversely, it is the limits, the fact that the facial muscles and their limits do not allow fully for what we wish to physically express that signals the loosening of the collective rules and cultural appropriations and acculturations that frighten us. We are so limited by our biology, and have been since the birth of this consciousness.

The compression and filtering of reality, the severance of the mind from that reality, so that it could not only survive but function, and thus dissolving itself from the immediate and infinite and into an intelligible understanding of reality which separates consciousness from reality - this compression and condensation and partitions of information are as a bottleneck both of our minds’ self-generating patterns, for example, as in errant or bizarre thoughts, as well as the information taken in.
