Contrary to our common sense and its posture as both an open embrace of true nature and an attitude of astonishment at the lack of perspectives thereof in others, nature doesn’t normally select for objective truth, but rather survival utility.

This is the medium by which we understand each other’s behavior, as individually many wish to be statuesque in fixed states of expression, but whose fluidity in duration is what we perceive from the outside, that is, as animals, yet we assume correctly based on our forms of communication that each of us has the same quality and depth of subjective experience. Statues of anger, joy, sadness, contemplation, boredom, but mostly predictability, and the rest. In sum, we leak, and are agitated continually by this, in the hesitations of speech, in the physical stumbling, in the mispronouncing and overcompensating emotions and emotive rationalizations that follow.

What if in the acknowledgment of our animal appearances, while obversely and simultaneously acknowledging our potentially infinite inner experiences, we also assumed that whatever processes of patterns of thoughts, the behavior of we animals would be the same? Because not only is that precisely what many are suggesting is at bottom, which would still leave the freedom of the mind’s awareness of being aware, but that the logical extension of determinism would suggest that epiphenomenalism would by necessity waste huge amounts of metabolic energy in various forms, most importantly regret. This overthinking and existential dread would have been selected against and extinguished long ago were it not functional, but moreover were it not sufficient in and of itself, though we, by our disassociation from duration and our bodies, must prove our free will to ourselves, which we might be able to do subjectively, but which is also readily apparent in ourselves as animals, as living organisms, as consciousness itself, in how we find ourselves in the way we organize reality and choose our way amidst the possibilities laid before us, often generated from our previous choices.

Though the ego is an illusion, the eye seeing its reflection in its own past eye watching itself, it is necessary both for future decisions based on its past experiences and the compression of data into intelligible patterns. Beyond that, this necessary illusion is as the silver film on the back of mirrors, allowing reflection back to itself and thus the universe.

In this text I have utilized mathematics, quantum mechanics, classical physics, chaos and information theories, fluid dynamics, statistics and probability theory, formal logic, thermodynamics, evolutionary biology, paleo-anthropology, psychology and poetry and philosophy and much more, all of which fall under the purview of language, and all of which I use as metaphors. Within language we are under no assumption that this form of communication reaches or touches reality, and therefore I have been using whatever metaphors are available to try and map the architecture of consciousness, which, in this text, I hope I have shown is impossible, at least completely and consistently and currently. Therefore, in this lightly holding of such heavy subjects, we are free to experiment and, once again, play as seriously as possible.

Back to the Cliffs of Dover and its measurement. If you were the cartographer trying to map that landscape, as we previously suggested, the more perceptive and the longer you spent peering and measuring into the self-repeating patterns of the coastline, making the measurement fit the rule, the measurement would approach infinity. Now look through the eyes of the map maker: eventually you would begin to see the coastline self-generating, creating without your input and measurement new inlets and crevices and even chalky white cliffs and even the fluctuating undulating water itself, whose fluidity itself defines the landscape. This is not simply an endorsement of looking inward into your own consciousness, though that is required, and what’s more, happening anyway. This is an incomplete description of reality itself and the limits of our perception. 

And internally, imagine you are that cartographer whose task is to map Van Gogh’s famous room, on a one to one ratio. You stand in the room drawing his chair and bed, the window, the yellow, the textures; but because you too are in the room you also must draw yourself drawing the map. And because consciousness is self-contained but open, when healthy, you now must draw yourself drawing yourself drawing the map, and so on. Because we are our consciousness, we cannot map it in its entirety while within that system. We need an outside perspective. And yet we are faced with a reality that will forever surpass our attempts to define it, that generates itself beyond our perception. 

In this way our never complete descriptions of reality and its ordering temporarily answers our foundational questions but folds in on itself again and seemingly forever, and this is something like the patterns of temperature quilts and their representations of information about the world, as well as the mediums by which they are known, namely, the quilts, the makers, the representations, and the facts about the temperature during various days or years.

——————-

Often we feel we are losing our independence and our identity with many people. Not only perhaps another coworker who you’ve spent too much time with, but also a romantic partner who you’ve spent years with. Almost immediately, we can imagine a dislike, accompanied by the attempts to think positively about the coworker. And as the years passed, and you slept in the same bed most nights with your partner, even their breathing and your own become so in sync that not only do you rarely notice it, but when they are tired you too feel the pull of dreams. Both the fighting against and the loving into are as becoming the other, each other, over time.

This is not only why friendships meld so well and are so fulfilling, but also why a few seem never to extinguish.

But we prey have evolved an equivalency of death with the unknown.

Interestingly, one of the reasons for our own desire for fixed states and fixed states of expressions, or short behavioral routines, is not only to make others feel safe, and therefore social cohesion, and of course to communicate, but so that we can communicate to others at all. We can only surmise what the other animals think, in their own way of thinking, when we perform. What other human animals see is not the full and fluid stories of our inner lives, and therefore these are most often misinterpreted in one form or another. But what they observe is us as an animal residing in time, a fluctuating and changing animal attempting to be both art and statue, perhaps more fluid even than an octopus. That is one of the central features in the dichotomy of individual conscious and the world it is conscious of.

The features on a face are such that, in our small repertoire of expressions, one can be thinking of disparate and altogether different ideas and feeling seemingly unrelated thoughts, yet still unfold the same raised eyebrow, or squint, or pursed lips, for different thought patterns.

That is how extreme the separation.

But this is all bypassed, mainly by the becoming within and of the worlds and its inhabitants, of which we are conscious. The fact that we get to be participant observers is this exchange of information, this becoming and transforming and expanding as this information, is a gift as natural as any of the animals. 

Observation is interactive. The errors allow for life and consciousness, which are spacio-temporal warriors battling to keep the universe, and themselves in it, and as it, alive. And we have evolved to regard that which is beyond our minds’ ocular culling and thus the unknown as dangerous, and so we render reality into manageable patterns, the myths that we are the apex predators, the knowers, the conquerors, the end of the story of our myths.

But as the continual wobble in the equation is necessary and essential for consciousness, so is the unknown essential for its continuance.

——————-

We must not mistake our own personal limits for the limits of others, nor the limits of other species, both in and off our planet, as evidenced by our own longing to transcend every border and bound and limits of our own. Some knew this of course, but they were few. Some of the ancients and myth makers and the beginning of philosophy and thus science. And Shakespeare of course. But the best most of us can do is play with pronunciations, with metaphors and analogies, exemplified by what I like to call the baby talk of many singers today. That is more appropriate than they know.

———————

In entropic closed systems, the entropy is due to lack of an exchange of energy and matter. For consciousness, that energy is information, which we organize and order into patterns which we can comprehend, and thus whose context is meaning. This is not simply self-referential closed loops of overwhelming data, but novel and complex and meaningful information, which generates its expansion out of systems of thought that crave certainty and encapsulations. The patterns as such, themselves, are both the understanding and how they are understood. We are ever learning to see more clearly, to perceive farther and with some depth.

Time defines those patterns, and those definitions are made of time through the arc between attention and intention, even as the body always sensorily attends while consciousness reflexively intends its attention, which informs its intending, and is then aware thereof. Free will here is not quite apt. We might instead say that we are freely attending, freely addressing and freely aware. Localized awareness is aware of something, and that something is the universe, which is also that which is aware.

Matter, time, consciousness and more are refractions of the same substance. They share the same physical and conceptual boundaries.

Consciousness as time and matter becomes aware of itself through an emergent property stemming from highly specific and complex arrangements of both time and matter, and time and matter expand outward in every direction exponentially faster, just as the universe currently is unfolding and opening infinitely into a boundless but self-contained system, whose nothing beyond the expansion and into which it expands, is the being of the source itself, and therefore also potentially everything.

Given enough time, time and matter become aware of themselves as itself, just as consciousness, itself aware of itself aware of itself ad infinitum, in infinite fractal recursions, which cannot prove nor fully not only not comprehend, but more importantly is unable to experience itself entirely, but whose awareness is boundless and open and self-contained, when healthy, taking in and releasing information, often in art forms, mostly in language, exchanging energy with its environment, all of which are patterns that the mind perceives and scissions and creates through attention and intention within and out of time, the context of which is moving (and there’s time again) matter, which itself is expressed as the absorption, reflection, transmission, and emission of the electromagnetic wavelength, affecting through its transparency or translucence or opacity how much vibration and oscillation excite atomic particles and generate heat or light, or, in this case, the colors and textures and sounds of our thoughts. The mind’s eye is like a kaleidoscope whose speed of light playing upon its plastic and glass determines the rate of time’s passing for the observer and through which only you can see, and whereof the need to share and communicate utilizes flawed approximations of reality, and the reality of the mind. But that attempt and need is the import and reason for expansion, as children create private languages, a mind and its every inner and secret thoughts which are just as real and sacred as anything else, as any star collapsing or exploding, as any physical and temporal act of love or violence or sunlight upon forearms or shared laughter at ridiculousness. And when you pass that instrument, that spyglass of geometric light and patterns to your companion, in the passing it rearranges itself through the movement so that your friend will always see what you saw always if but slightly differently. No one has ever seen your particular vantage point, ever, in this existence. No one has harnessed and employed and built itself out of time itself the way you have, which then becomes relative to your individual awareness. And it is as new and spectacular to the universe as well, seeing through your eyes and thoughts.

As we are forever listening to the dark, the endless, the mystery, so are we too, like the universe, both overwhelmed by our senses and sensorily deprived in our minute awareness, and so we create just as the universe creates, reflecting on and within ourselves, and this localized scale of fractal recursions is infinitely recorded as not only information aware of itself, but the universe aware of itself. 

The universe is the mirror in which we witness ourselves. The world that we are conscious of, and all of its inhabitants, and the universe containing it, is the delimitations by which we observe our own reflection. And the universe witnesses and takes notes and records and is aware through the mirrors of our own minds, that is, our mind’s eye seeing itself in the reflections of the past mind’s eye witnessing. The source glimpses itself through our very perception.

———————

As it is possible that the extension past the crucibles of heat and energy and into chaotic equilibrium may regenerate through radiating singularities, or even renormalize and transcend itself entirely, so the zero point abstractions of consciousness fluctuate in and out of existence into potentially infinite, and potentially infinitely expanding awareness as a self-generating organization of specific repetitions in and of space and time, and there patterns are both ordering and are ordered perception that keeps both the individual and overall system open, keeping the universe alive through life and perception.

One of the many reasons I love this song of life, and, of course, the music itself, which like any language, like that of mathematics or poetry or any other, is not only metaphorical in and of itself, in that reality ever self-generates beyond the symbolic capacity to map it, but is defined by its limits and boundaries, where silence defines substance, where the shape of the pattern is defined by what it is not, and in this music we are so moved even by this incompleteness, this attempt to express and reflect the terror and awe of existence, that we can’t help but physically move with the beat and rhythm, is that we dance even while sitting, as babies learn to dance before they walk.

If you simply read some of this text casually, it might sound like postmodern phenomenological and subjective foo foo and nonsense. But like everything in this text, it is an attempt to use incomplete and inadequate tools to measure reality. As you may have noticed by now, every analogy in this book, the violin, Dover, squids and the octopus and the rest, the mathematics and physics and biology and poetry, are applicable to every other analogy and metaphor and idea within this system, as in water and hands and music and consciousness and time and the universe. In this way the text is self-contained and fractal, but remains open and amenable to change and updates and counter arguments and counter factuals and growth. You as the participant observer, that is, the active reader, may yet always inform.

Still, we have now been going inward in the spiral, and the circumference is tightening and words are getting abstract beyond comprehension. Although if dissected carefully much can be gained. Still, abstractions just beyond this point would become something different for each reader.

And so I tentatively and temporarily finish this experiment by suggesting that not only do you witness your own awareness in the mirror of the universe, but that the universe reciprocates the exact function in your mirror, or consciousness. 

Given the chance, life will arise and explode and thrive exponentially throughout its environment, until its environment deems otherwise, or until it surpasses and moves on from its environment. Look at the gargantuan amount of life on and in this planet, the sheer biomass in the oceans and in the earth. The prochlorococcus, the nematodes, the ants. I was just sitting in the grass in our backyard, the wind dying down after two weeks and the sun warm in this low desert, and sitting next to the wonderful burl in the trunk of one of the apple trees I watched the ants and spiders and insects flitting about. I put my hand on the flagstone to my right, and then the clay pot, and felt their warmth. The air smelled of our pines and flowers and the living. In the movements of life I found myself content contemplating these beings, these flits of consciousness. And I drank some water and came in to write this.

It is an immense responsibility and freedom and gift. Every thought matters and is therefrom as sacred as existence itself, and perhaps this regard will allow for acceptance and commitment to the possible eternal unfolding of every choice, as every thought is recorded and immortalized.

May you remain insatiably curious. 

I am off to learn. To battle and love and experience against the onslaught of the senses and becoming undone and the forward marching of time toward my death and the death of everything. My consciousness opens and unfurls in fractal geometric patterns backward to keep the universe alive, creating even as existence creates, which is the mirror, mine own mind’s eye witnessing itself recursively, recording itself in perpetuity, each and every thought an immortal and endless record and pattern of the whole open but self-contained existence itself, which is exactly mirrored in my own consciousness, which is the universe ever becoming aware of itself, localized in and as me, this laughing and dancing ape, this at times crying and angry and self-conscious and grumpy and overwhelmed and over-confident primate, this maker of mistakes, this over-thinker, who everyday looks more like an ape scratching himself and looking about. One eyebrow cocked. A little worried. A little too earnest and sincere. But playing and honest most of the time.

And I coo to you in our infancy songs not yet written.

sources.title: Parables of Silence, Preface, Why A Thought Experiment?.txt
sources.content:
Adi Shankaracharya: “The whole universe is a superimposition [of form] on the Brahman although it seems to be separate from it... Consciousness or Awareness is the substratum.
consciousness. There is no place where God is not."
— Swami Rama
“My eye and God's eye is one eye, and one sight, and one knowledge, and one love.
Meister Eckhart
“For the mind, there is no true separation from God. Even though it looks like you are separated, you are always connected. God or Consciousness is always a whole, it is One."
— Gardener
“God knows us from within ourselves, not as objects, not as strangers, not as intimates, but as our own selves."
— Jesuit Resource
“For God is himself the Being of all Beings, and we are as gods in him, through whom he revealeth himself.”
Jakob Bohme
“I am no longer the wave of consciousness thinking itself separated from the sea of cosmic consciousness. I am the ocean of Spirit that has become the wave of human life.”
Paramahansa Yogananda
"In him [Man], for the first time on earth, consciousness has coiled back upon itself to become thought".
Pierre Teilhard de Chardin
“We believe that the universe itself is conscious in a way that we can never truly understand. It is engaged in a search for meaning. So it breaks itself apart, investing its own consciousness in every form of life. We are the universe trying to understand itself."
Delenn ("Passing Through Gethsemane"),      Babylon 5
“We are a way for the cosmos to know itself."
— Carl Sagan, Cosmos
“Through our eyes, the universe is perceiving itself. Through our ears, the universe is listening to its harmonies. We are the witnesses through which the universe becomes conscious of its glory, of its magnificence."
— Alan Watts
“We are the cosmos made conscious and life is the means by which the universe understands itself."
— Brian Cox
“You are an aperture through which the universe is looking at and exploring itself."
— Alan Watts
“The cosmos is within us. We are made of star-stuff."
— Carl Sagan
“A human being is part of the Universe... He experiences himself... as something separate, a kind of optical delusion of his consciousness."
— Albert Einstein
All of these quotes came from an approximately ten minute Google search. The idea that the universe has become aware of itself in the form of us is at least almost 3,000-years-old, as evidenced by the 	Sat-chit-ananda, and most likely much, much older. It is an idea that we stumble upon over and over throughout history. It is an honest attempt in most cases while deliberating to describe or explain or interpret that for which we have no proof. This is as interesting as existence itself, and also not all that interesting, because it falls into those things which can neither be proven nor disproved, what I call metaphysics, since the topics and concepts which come after physics or beyond reality as we know it and for which we can find no evidence are generally matters of faith. And anecdotal evidence is often simplistic and suspect.
They can also be a matter of exploration. God, universal consciousness, Atman, the Source, the Abyss, a metavariable, the singularity, are all one of those concepts such that it is an idea of eternity that we fit inside a metaphor that itself points beyond comprehension. Here we will delve into that and other metaphysical concepts, using as analogies and metaphors ideas from physics and mathematics and information and chaos theories and poetry and philosophy and evolutionary biology and more.
As such, you will find herein many, many references to authors and ideas. Not all of them are cited when there is no need for direct quotation, and that is to both commit to the tempo and process of the ideas unfolding in the book, as well as avoiding quotidian platitudes and unnecessary explanatory length, instead of defining the concepts laid bare here. See? I just did it there. Many of these concepts are exceptionally well known, but I either utilize them in novel ways or refute or apply them. If you perchance come upon a source that I have neglected to properly acknowledge, my apologies. In the mining of our cultural knowledge I may very well have missed where I myself have learned some information, and mistaken it for either universal or mine own ideas. But where there is a simple suggestion of context, there it is assumed that you as an active participant will know the nod, or will trace the idea back to its roots. These are everywhere throughout, and the pebbles where we walk this experiment.
The tools and their techniques that can be utilized from looking at these concepts as if they were facts, while suspending judgment on their veracity, is what I find most interesting. If we are to measure anything at all, let’s measure the efficacy of these thoughts. Let’s throw all of our capacity and talents upon the environment herein, internally and externally, as seriously and playfully as we are able. In the process we will remember older and newer wisdoms, which we will be mining for as much perception as possible. (By the way, serious play, which I perhaps remind the reader too much of in these pages, was legally coined by Lego in the mid-nineties, but I first encountered it as a description of Plato’s technique from the interpretive essay in the back of Alan Bloom’s translation of The Republic, who may have found a like term in Jung. And that’s how it all works - everything ties and that binds and makes up the tapestry of our inner lives. We call this the collective consciousness.)
This is a study of the architecture of consciousness. It is not academic, nor spiritual, nor religious. It is a thought experiment to see how elastic the boundaries of thought and how focused we can be and close the inspection. It will come off as circular and dense and at first, a bit of a slog. As we go along it will become smoother, sleeker. Upon every recursion to a topic there will be new perspectives. There is a reason for all of this. There are patterns throughout, even as we describe patterns. But I’m giving the show away.
I invite you along to actively participate in this discussion and story, and not simply to be a passive recipient. That belies the project. Approaching this experiment with rigidity or the need for answers will be fruitless. The project of the experiment is nothing less than trying to grasp some of the biggest and acutest ideas we currently employ and sometimes labor with.
This is not entertainment, nor instructive. Together we will be joyfully hunting and sniffing out patterns and relationships, circling the prey, who is us, mostly defenseless without tools, and those tools are defined and used by and through their limits and restrictions, as resistance and flow allows most birds to fly.
What we think are traps may in fact be instruments.
——————-
Imagine you are sitting outside in the darkness of the early morning hours, unable to sleep. You have the earbuds in and are listening to music while you think, in order not to bother the neighbors. A noise startles you. It matters not what noise. It could be the sirens in some rap songs, or the crashing of thunder in others, or an off-tempo drum beat that you mistake for a noise made around the corner or over the wall. But then you pay attention after a heart skip and slight panic. You pay attention to the music. You focus. You expand your awareness at the same time to listen beyond the music to your environs. You become alert, and the model of your perception of reality’s truth and honesty is measured by its fidelity to the unknown. That is what is required of you here. I invite you to come with me and play, as seriously and as rigorously and honestly as possible. Here will not be answers in the ways you may think of them, but perhaps an interesting regard toward that unknown, as well as a hope for the animal that has everything to learn.
———————-
Predictive ability of the future and not simply the anticipation of reliable repetitions of unfolding patterns, predictions which mean safety, and thus the reason for our prey species sculpting time, becomes more accurate the more vague it is and on ever longer timelines, although with shorter spans based on intelligent data it can be more accurate, though always uncertain. Evidence of extrasensory abilities can never be replicated by will, or else someone would have repeatedly and often won a million dollars by now and beat all the tests, and any attempt to do so will undoubtedly lead to embarrassment. The attitude and its project and fallibility and openness of science remains the best way to evaluate, measure and comprehend the universe, at least for now, even though we make what we measure fit the ruling instruments, a kind of formal curiosity and potent and operational formulation of honest inquiry, at least one of the reasons because anyone can witness the evidence for themselves, at any time, for the most part. Yet we don’t. We do not normally measure and test the tensile strength of a bridge we are about to cross, nor most things that we encounter and use throughout the day. We rely heavily and mostly on expert testimony. We are largely epistemically dependent.
I've been up since a little after midnight. I wish I could experience a linear timeline of self-awareness, but perhaps I never could. When I was a child I called it the quickening, where everything would begin to happen and move too fast, and I felt kind of cold and fuzzy around the edges. I understand why so many of the men in my life have been so quiet, so reserved, finding a corner of the world within themselves where nothing can be misinterpreted, where nothing is caused, yet still standing at ready, to protect, to labor. Things for me now move in spirals, and I stand or fall or rise in their centers, noncommittal, watching, knowing I know so very little, like a high-wire act in a storm, a squall on the palm, ready to protect, to labor, where time and stories can go both ways, and are circular and widen their circumference.
Say you are an older woman who is trying to set up a match between a young man and woman, and you need to know the age of the man. You need information, and, so it turns, the information comes up naturally in conversation, all because you had or set up a feeling of intention. But the feeling has patterns too, whose stories we can read. And it is a sophisticated and elegant planner and organizer and player who matches moves ahead, after every reset from variation, who understands statistical errors and sets courses and follows through. One of the things we will establish as a rule to the game we are to play, one that cannot have nor needs winners, is that everything is patterns. And patterns have shapes and content which can be read. Patterns are information whose context is meaning. More, patterns can be felt. Most people experience their really internal workings as emotions, which we then narrate unto ourselves. The spilt happens or spills out of the body as well as into the body, like the wet as a byproduct of the Kicksey-Winsey, keeping everything in check so that castles and magic can float in the sky.
It is all consciousness.

sources.title: Title Page.txt
sources.content:
Parables of Silence:
A Thought Experiment 

J. E. Cassella



A smile winces, crumbling below my left cheek
where humming inside an ochre flame barely 
tries hiding scores of prose,

quickening, drumming gross thread, listening 
to cracking embers where I have placed us 
cross-legged 
around firestones, birdstones of men,
where I would grow me twigs and dancing set 
your being to root.
